name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: Ideas, debate endure.
body:
News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

Stay up to date on all of this and more when you[ subscribe to our daily email list ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=62828d9dff&e=b7b3eddce2)and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1.[We must not become indifferent](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=7dc9e684e6&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Guy Ciarrocchi_
Charlie Kirk was assassinated. We do not yet know the murderer, but we have a strong sense of why this dad and husband was gunned down.

I was on my way to an AI discussion in Pittsburgh on how Pittsburgh and all of Pennsylvania could become a national leader in AI technology, jobs and innovation — to talk with people of various ideologies, expertise and insights, who share a common interest and common goals.

Then my wife texted me. She texted our family group chat. I texted with friends, fellow commentators, and thought leaders across our region, and nation. I prayed for his recovery. I prayed for his soul. I cried. I prayed, again.

**Why It Matters**. It hurts us more because we live in the world of ideas. Because we believe in the American experiment. Because we want to debate. To make our points. To hear others. To resolve problems. To advance America. This hurts not only because it’s so unfair and so extreme. It’s because it’s a blow to the American experiment. We have to look to our faith in God. And to our belief in America and push on. And hug our family.

Charlie Kirk — a dad, a husband, a thought leader — was gunned down, mostly likely because of what he loved to do: educate, debate and promote the American experiment.

We must redouble our efforts to be active, engaged citizens — and to love our families.

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=77a214d9ae&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2.[Philly School District spent at least $309,000 sending staff to local union conference despite travel policy](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=17e108ec26&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Todd Shepherd_
The School District of Philadelphia spent at least $309,000 for several hundred of its employees to attend a union conference in March, according to documents obtained via a Right to Know Law request. Many of the expenses conflict with the district’s own travel policies.
About $294,000 went to registration fees at $440–$500 per person, and roughly $14,500 covered about 40 hotel rooms, bringing the total to about $309,000 — even though the conference was located in Philadelphia.
If the district has a rationale for the expenses, or if it disputes the totals, it did not respond to a lengthy request for comment from Broad + Liberty. Nothing in the current CASA[ union contract](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=af18c8fe0b&e=b7b3eddce2) would seem to require the district to directly pay for or support union-led conferences, and the district did not point to any contract provision requiring it to pay for such conferences.
**Why It Matters.** At a minimum, the hotel expenses would seem to conflict with part of the district’s travel policies, one of which urges employees to attend conferences by virtual means, seemingly with expenses in mind. "Employees who seek reimbursement for conference attendance shall choose virtual conference attendance, if that is available, or choose the most local location for in-person attendance if virtual attendance is not an option,”[ the policy says](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=3229b006d0&e=b7b3eddce2). Yet another policy addresses the point more specifically,[ saying](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=683da3e393&e=b7b3eddce2), “An overnight stay is not permitted within the greater Philadelphia region unless the Superintendent or their designee grants an exception[.]”
If “exception” forms were created, they were not turned over in response to the Right to Know request.
**[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=59a4c838fd&e=b7b3eddce2)**
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[Beth Ann Rosica: Teacher attrition — student behavior and school safety drive ongoing crisis](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ad7866fce0&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Brian Wink: William Penn and the right to free speech](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=61bf48c683&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Ben Mannes: Can the National Guard stop crime beyond D.C.?](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4646204f4e&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Thom Nickels: Life, death, and poetry on the Frankford El](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=8571a1b090&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Paul Davis: Masked marauders — dealing with raccoons in the city](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=579dc87506&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Stew Bolno: Trump’s business experience is the secret to his success](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4def4f3b6b&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Ada Nestor: Voters will soon get their say on Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=59f52df1bc&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
|
# **4\. What we're reading**
All we — and most anyone else — have been reading this week have been about the horrific assassination of Charlie Kirk on Wednesday in Utah. But more than the analysis of the killer and his crime, we would choose to focus on the many tributes penned by those who knew Kirk well, and those whose lives were touched by him. Adam Rubenstein’s article, “[The Charlie Kirk I Knew](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=3ddfe22b07&e=b7b3eddce2),” is one. Ben Shapiro’s “[The Assassination of Charlie Kirk and the Fight for America’s Soul](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=13f08de940&e=b7b3eddce2),” is another. We should all reflect on the man, his mission, and the hope that America can collectively pull back from this perilous precipice.
---
|
---
|
# **5\. Download our app**
If you’re reading this, you already know we’re the only place you can find certain stories in Pennsylvania—the ones other outlets won’t touch, the ones that dig deeper, and the straightforward political news that keeps you ahead of the spin. Now, we’re bringing that news straight to you — faster, clearer, and without the noise.

The _Broad + Liberty_ mobile app is here, and they’re the fastest way to get Pennsylvania’s real news. [Read all about it here](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=38e7938af5&e=b7b3eddce2)!

clean_body: News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | | | | | Good morning and welcome to Broad + Liberty 's Weekly Reads. Stay up to date on all of this and more when you get the best of Broad + Liberty delivered straight to your inbox. | | 1. By Guy Ciarrocchi Charlie Kirk was assassinated. We do not yet know the murderer, but we have a strong sense of why this dad and husband was gunned down. I was on my way to an AI discussion in Pittsburgh on how Pittsburgh and all of Pennsylvania could become a national leader in AI technology, jobs and innovation — to talk with people of various ideologies, expertise and insights, who share a common interest and common goals. Then my wife texted me. She texted our family group chat. I texted with friends, fellow commentators, and thought leaders across our region, and nation. I prayed for his recovery. I prayed for his soul. I cried. I prayed, again. Why It Matters. It hurts us more because we live in the world of ideas. Because we believe in the American experiment. Because we want to debate. To make our points. To hear others. To resolve problems. To advance America. This hurts not only because it’s so unfair and so extreme. It’s because it’s a blow to the American experiment. We have to look to our faith in God. And to our belief in America and push on. And hug our family. Charlie Kirk — a dad, a husband, a thought leader — was gunned down, mostly likely because of what he loved to do: educate, debate and promote the American experiment. We must redouble our efforts to be active, engaged citizens — and to love our families. | | 2. By Todd Shepherd The School District of Philadelphia spent at least $309,000 for several hundred of its employees to attend a union conference in March, according to documents obtained via a Right to Know Law request. Many of the expenses conflict with the district’s own travel policies. About $294,000 went to registration fees at $440–$500 per person, and roughly $14,500 covered about 40 hotel rooms, bringing the total to about $309,000 — even though the conference was located in Philadelphia. If the district has a rationale for the expenses, or if it disputes the totals, it did not respond to a lengthy request for comment from Broad + Liberty. Nothing in the current CASA would seem to require the district to directly pay for or support union-led conferences, and the district did not point to any contract provision requiring it to pay for such conferences. Why It Matters. At a minimum, the hotel expenses would seem to conflict with part of the district’s travel policies, one of which urges employees to attend conferences by virtual means, seemingly with expenses in mind. "Employees who seek reimbursement for conference attendance shall choose virtual conference attendance, if that is available, or choose the most local location for in-person attendance if virtual attendance is not an option,”. Yet another policy addresses the point more specifically,, “An overnight stay is not permitted within the greater Philadelphia region unless the Superintendent or their designee grants an exception[.]” If “exception” forms were created, they were not turned over in response to the Right to Know request. | | 3\. Lightning Round | | 4\. What we're reading All we — and most anyone else — have been reading this week have been about the horrific assassination of Charlie Kirk on Wednesday in Utah. But more than the analysis of the killer and his crime, we would choose to focus on the many tributes penned by those who knew Kirk well, and those whose lives were touched by him. Adam Rubenstein’s article, “,” is one. Ben Shapiro’s “,” is another. We should all reflect on the man, his mission, and the hope that America can collectively pull back from this perilous precipice. | | 5\. Download our app If you’re reading this, you already know we’re the only place you can find certain stories in Pennsylvania—the ones other outlets won’t touch, the ones that dig deeper, and the straightforward political news that keeps you ahead of the spin. Now, we’re bringing that news straight to you — faster, clearer, and without the noise. The Broad + Liberty mobile app is here, and they’re the fastest way to get Pennsylvania’s real news. ! | | | Broad + Liberty is funded by readers like you. Your generous support ensures that Broad + Liberty can bring you stories and opinions that Pennsylvania’s mainstream media would rather leave unheard. Please consider making a contribution today. If you would like your gift to be used to help us cover a specific story or subject, please let us know in the contribution form or at mailto:editirs@broadandliberty.com. As always, gifts made to Broad + Liberty are 100% tax deductible! Thank you, dear reader, for your steadfast support of our independent, broad-minded brand of local journalism. We could not do this without you. With gratitude, — The Editors at Broad + Liberty | | "Support Broad + Liberty" | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2025 Broad + Liberty, All rights reserved. name: Mikie Sherrill
subject: Here’s the deal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| Hey, I know my team and I have been emailing you a lot recently asking for your support. But I wanted to take a second to explain why your support matters in this crucial race.

Not only is my opponent, Jack Ciattarelli, endorsed by Donald Trump – he’s also a faithful advocate for the most disastrous elements of Trump’s agenda, and wants to implement them here in New Jersey. He was one of the biggest cheerleaders for the Republican price hike bill that will throw hundreds of thousands of New Jerseyans off their health care.

And now, Ciattarelli’s being supported by a MAGA super PAC that’s pouring _millions of dollars_ into negative ads that make one false assertion after another about my record and my character.

[In a race this close and with the stakes this high, we need to have the resources to be able to fight back, and we can only do that with your support. Can you split $10 (or whatever you can) between my campaign and the Voter Protection Project so we can stop our opponent and protect New Jersey?](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93926?t=6&akid=21218%2E878179%2EaH1L_h) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Mikie Sherrill and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93927?t=7&akid=21218%2E878179%2EaH1L_h)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93928?t=8&akid=21218%2E878179%2EaH1L_h)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93929?t=9&akid=21218%2E878179%2EaH1L_h)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93930?t=10&akid=21218%2E878179%2EaH1L_h)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93943?t=11&akid=21218%2E878179%2EaH1L_h)
But this race isn’t about what we’re fighting against — it’s about what we’re fighting _for._

**My vision for our state is simple: Make life better for New Jerseyans.** I’ll fight to bring health care, housing, child care, and energy costs down, tackle crime and keep our communities safe, and defend our constitutional freedoms from the right-wing extremists who are taking our nation down a destructive path.

Here’s the deal — we’re in an extremely competitive race, but lots of voters still haven’t made up their minds.[ Your support helps us spread our message across New Jersey, so we can build the coalition we need to win this race. That’s why I’m humbly asking you to split a contribution between my campaign and the Voter Protection Project — it’ll make a critical difference in helping us protect New Jersey.](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93926?t=12&akid=21218%2E878179%2EaH1L_h)

Mikie

And now, Ciattarelli’s being supported by a MAGA super PAC that’s pouring millions of dollars into negative ads that make one false assertion after another about my record and my character. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Mikie Sherrill and VPP will go through immediately: | But this race isn’t about what we’re fighting against — it’s about what we’re fighting for. My vision for our state is simple: Make life better for New Jerseyans. I’ll fight to bring health care, housing, child care, and energy costs down, tackle crime and keep our communities safe, and defend our constitutional freedoms from the right-wing extremists who are taking our nation down a destructive path. Here’s the deal — we’re in an extremely competitive race, but lots of voters still haven’t made up their minds. Thank you, Mikie | | Paid for by VPP (www.protectvoting.org) Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104 The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps. Please, consider making a . Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a. to request fewer emails. 📈 **Investment Update:_DLCC Invests Nearly $1.5M in Virginia_ **
This week, the DLCC announced an investment of nearly $1.5 million to support the Virginia House Democratic Caucus and the Democratic nominee for Virginia Lieutenant Governor. DLCC is committed to protecting and expanding Virginia Democrats’ House majority. [Learn more about our investment → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hWwpbRT_BYJsLvsW0RbBHsbB9yaFgoGHOQI6j6xtLJS9ZVzwBQChveqnyrf8IkjgKNfjVeLNP4cixXTFd1eZITDP-mPrQhoQFrPyFtPAY6XybnznxgeWlq4G98Ha407f2_SN6RrdNXGWZk92I0XnCIHMQqz2aLjEFCWrNeibSxcIdgOUrTa5ivQ_Yy1IVpQ8KVrJS_HUD3t1ZWqaE-XnBbA2sfxYM5O3OdM-vKiYYE1SeLAUi_IOET5SoGbtdF0NwuRs4zvhKSo8QBC9uNkbG6AYJtL5quCCFD9CQ6qi4xODx4Pt55c_m2rHeEru6N-2sdxlut-3bTO-SLgMGCK0Fd11GXIAJmAjrgHG5IRK5eWcXGxqMnsV-1yJ7B_8JUpik_vbBQi71hri7RW3l40qAGhBvRIQVMs3jDFjzq5n8EXNPqef3xep0d9xqjvNlxK5GMSVxyQ_EVPntRtSayAdIF1_qvNqBaiaVEYmeoqCuNQWf1tTdrpmGWtQ4OHQSeKI2aRsjpU3MaFYFOZsTiC-rZdD36MMuphiBSJtLKUXHq-L7MPTPqog5QKg5_McgNUyBsUdCVR1zuxdFDG-cT_eUYo5w8301Xgkpxl6K3Fo7x0bH_eHQoCq4KP_PmR4sXd3L1XiWG9VDyfXi5xGrGa7ReoA/4jw/5Y2nDfdsSUCTD8yh9i25PA/h1/h001.renH1EHRT2qavSGg6HWlD9DAyo_MqWPrv6mVFuqGH_o)

👥 **Missouri:_The Republican-controlled House advances a bill to make it harder for voters to amend the state constitution_ **
Under the proposal, constitutional amendments put on the ballot by voters would need a simple majority statewide and a majority in all eight congressional districts to pass. This means that a small percentage of the state’s voters could defeat any ballot measure. [Learn more here → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.A3cd-kVlFz1CzUMSYeTvVahC7-MiOQbqHPNXCKa3I9OX0_Ux5aXYR6Ca2J5clKRnaBuUm9sMqOaNxs4V0szQSox_N5-GECUOYXwnFmQQWuZ5CDeqYs4a8Dzx9wA9pcJ5zhwiPMn11FuFG8glfnS56NEIUE-VRcuTwhr_rnrNP8yjS-UnGio0gjc27Qx8GTk1HdU2SJqr-bs3HYQMDDCNkUaZGZEBhR9OCeJBKQgCVVJYmZKp-WUh2ep3uUl4EuVYJ3gJNuobNPMl3zizHyOruWzcJKLL9ncv7g0yt0ZUsrhFtGNoeDGitnaXn2cYT5QiZ8beOFxhRGqz7PsEMuFOLycfivDWbv7vypvHtk66ec824HYCNnfHb3tRrumjyGiDpVJWBH_ZxXkmZ7k75HWCWSKGe4QGMmN77J81s9q-YMcQwEIeM8Eujs5RwuJtDpPp19Yyf_sDJUD-Pa7XWq9RUACnFTDkEt62QNc_qcoztwjVsYbPEGp11_KZKbIh6Uhuu44ePQWXhyMU1JSPlNCQaHxWJEe-JELYn4ZAw1lOFk6pfMyyl09qIcfz6LYBmenvrNotnJi2hkx6ZTxVOAlqnjv423RzAQJcmZ1zOTrSOcsspI_LQc081ea9rSkXqTNrm3HWMX0oF-Gh8pAkh2KUywjhsfr69JEk6zXS3Jukx9w/4jw/5Y2nDfdsSUCTD8yh9i25PA/h3/h001.BnXoOajb7EP82Z7q9GwISRn29b0BhNVuPZOoCyDOyEQ)

State Democrats are facing nearly 200 elections in the coming months, and a runoff election in less than two weeks. ### Champion of Free Speech and Faith Charlie Kirk Assassinated
---
| |
---
| The tragic loss of Charlie Kirk is a profound national tragedy that has left us all heartbroken. At just 31 years old, this devoted father leaves behind his wife, Erika, and their two young children, who will grow up without his guidance simply because he dared to voice a differing opinion. Charlie was peacefully seated on stage at Utah Valley University, engaging in an open debate as part of his "American Comeback Tour," when an assassin's bullet ended his life on September 10, 2025, an act of senseless political violence that has shocked the nation. We have been holding his family in our prayers throughout this devastating week, seeking comfort for their unimaginable grief.
Tonight, there will be a community Prayer Vigil in honor of Charlie Kirk at 5 p.m. on the Courthouse Lawn in Goochland County, Virginia. This gathering is an opportunity to come together in unity, reflection, and prayer, echoing the many vigils springing up across the country to remember his legacy.
Charlie Kirk was a tireless advocate for free speech, open debate, and civil discourse. Through Turning Point USA, he created platforms where even his fiercest critics could voice opposing views without fear. Yet, in a heartbreaking act of domestic terrorism, he was assassinated by 22-year-old suspect Tyler Robinson, who reportedly harbored disdain for Kirk's conservative viewpoints. Tragically, following his death, many on the radical left have responded with victim-blaming rhetoric and dehumanizing celebrations, underscoring the deep divisions that Charlie fought so hard to bridge. There message is clear, keep different points of views to yourself or else.
Above all, Charlie Kirk was a man of deep faith who loved our Savior Jesus Christ, cherished his family above everything, and was passionately devoted to our country. He courageously pushed back against the radical left's divisive ideologies that have eroded our national unity. His life was cut short for simply exercising his First Amendment rights, a stark reminder of the fragility of our freedoms.
Rage is Not the Only Answer
In stark contrast to the unrest we've seen in other tragedies, Charlie's supporters are responding with mourning and resolve, not riots, looting, or destruction. Let us honor his example by committing to the civility he championed.
Charlie Kirk's funeral will be held next Sunday, September 21, in Arizona, where friends, family, and admirers from across the nation will gather to celebrate a life that inspired millions. In this time of sorrow, may we rededicate ourselves to the values Charlie held dear: faith, family, freedom, and fearless truth-telling. Our thoughts and prayers remain with Erika and the children, may God's peace surround them.
[Visit the Turning Point USA website here to learn more and get involved in the movement.](https://eky6fg9ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wwS5RqEdCM440XzjVmFjkG_Gp73LPeNvmsSkc-2xZUuRKNfwMAbiCFlXyIQbds71UPR_bKH2wpyf_DGTcyfEatxjpLQYs4oyiU7UJepaKh1nX4CvKSy0vxpyvSw1v_egNEuqgNshj7upvMmX_OojMA==&c=3ZAhewVYyEkNf_FQyeF4g2tUoicyaEg4dTXakSnt5wKrh5J7P-Ecxw==&ch=ONvLjtxsCxOGrFCqDFfihJhcBuWoH6-QdHj9A5HQwtyTin-Q7KLGyA==)

| Erika Kirk delivered a moving and heartfelt message about her deep love for her remarkable husband, Charlie Kirk. He had the potential to be an extraordinary president of the United States one day. Tragically, Charlie's children will grow up without their father. Though this loss is profound, I trust that God will bring good from this sorrow, even in the midst of a difficult week. Charlie's unwavering commitment to loving Jesus Christ and upholding the founding principles of our nation will only grow stronger, inspiring countless others to carry his vision forward.
My prayer is that his legacy and message will continue to draw our country closer to God, fostering a brighter future for all Americans. | |

| Common Sense Over Nonsense
---
| Team,
Early voting begins in just 5 days, on September 19th! Let's spread the word to friends, family, and neighbors to vote Republican and support our shared values of God, Family, the Constitution, Freedom, a strong business climate, and common-sense policies.
Recently, every House Democrat in Washington, D.C., voted against the Working Family Tax Cut Act, a bill that would have prevented the largest tax increase on the middle class in history, supported law and order, codified 28 Trump Executive Orders, promoted safe cities, and fueled economic growth.
Instead, Democrats, led by figures like Spanberger, continue to push policies that defy reason and morality, including:

* Allowing boys in girls' bathrooms and sports
* Supporting gender surgeries for minors
* Advocating open borders
* Releasing dangerous criminals onto our streets
* Defunding the police

Our job as legislators is to be your voice and get things done. I've been able to pass bills each year where every republican and every democrat voted yes, but the policies supported by Spanberger stand in stark contrast to our commitment to law-abiding citizens and Christian values. We must reject the normalization of harmful ideas and ask: Why do Democrats prioritize criminals over hardworking families?
Our team has been tirelessly knocking on doors, funding campaigns across Virginia, putting up signs, and greeting voters across the 5th Congressional District. All three statewide offices and all 100 House of Delegates seats are up for grabs this November. Winsome Sears and our full Republican ticket are aligned on the issues that matter, but victory depends on turning out the vote.
For Freedom and Common Sense, I urge you to vote early on September 19th. This will free you up to help us rally others to the polls. Together, we can secure a brighter future for our district and our nation.
Here are some photos from around the district and some of what we have been up to:

From helping organize every Trump rally in Virginia to championing the entire Republican ticket, Ramona's commitment has never been about "me"; it's always been about "we."
Ramona and her team's example is exactly what we need to secure victory this November.
President Trump, Chris LaCivita, and the people of Virginia's 5th trust in Ramona Christian. For all things political for John McGuire in District 5, please send Ramona Christian an email to ramona@mcguireva.com

| Sen. Tim Kaine's recent remarks exposed a fundamental truth about the modern Democratic Party: they reject the principle that our inalienable rights are endowed by our Creator.
The United States was founded on the belief that our rights, enshrined in the Constitution, are God-given and inherent to every individual. Yet, Kaine's comments suggest Democrats view these rights as privileges bestowed by the government. This is a profound and dangerous distinction. It reveals a worldview where rights are not sacred but subject to the whims of those in power.
If Democrats believe the government grants our rights, they could just as easily revoke them upon regaining control. This perspective undermines the very foundation of our republic. Our Founding Fathers fought the Revolutionary War to reject tyranny and affirm that our liberties are not contingent on any ruler, regime, or political party.
Our rights are not privileges to be dispensed or withdrawn by a dictator, monarch, or government. They are unalienable, endowed by God, and must be defended as such.

clean_body: | | | | God Bless America! | | | == | == | Champion of Free Speech and Faith Charlie Kirk Assassinated | | | The tragic loss of Charlie Kirk is a profound national tragedy that has left us all heartbroken. At just 31 years old, this devoted father leaves behind his wife, Erika, and their two young children, who will grow up without his guidance simply because he dared to voice a differing opinion. Charlie was peacefully seated on stage at Utah Valley University, engaging in an open debate as part of his "American Comeback Tour," when an assassin's bullet ended his life on September 10, 2025, an act of senseless political violence that has shocked the nation. We have been holding his family in our prayers throughout this devastating week, seeking comfort for their unimaginable grief. Tonight, there will be a community Prayer Vigil in honor of Charlie Kirk at 5 p.m. on the Courthouse Lawn in Goochland County, Virginia. This gathering is an opportunity to come together in unity, reflection, and prayer, echoing the many vigils springing up across the country to remember his legacy. Charlie Kirk was a tireless advocate for free speech, open debate, and civil discourse. Through Turning Point USA, he created platforms where even his fiercest critics could voice opposing views without fear. Yet, in a heartbreaking act of domestic terrorism, he was assassinated by 22-year-old suspect Tyler Robinson, who reportedly harbored disdain for Kirk's conservative viewpoints. Tragically, following his death, many on the radical left have responded with victim-blaming rhetoric and dehumanizing celebrations, underscoring the deep divisions that Charlie fought so hard to bridge. There message is clear, keep different points of views to yourself or else. Above all, Charlie Kirk was a man of deep faith who loved our Savior Jesus Christ, cherished his family above everything, and was passionately devoted to our country. He courageously pushed back against the radical left's divisive ideologies that have eroded our national unity. His life was cut short for simply exercising his First Amendment rights, a stark reminder of the fragility of our freedoms. Rage is Not the Only Answer In stark contrast to the unrest we've seen in other tragedies, Charlie's supporters are responding with mourning and resolve, not riots, looting, or destruction. Let us honor his example by committing to the civility he championed. Charlie Kirk's funeral will be held next Sunday, September 21, in Arizona, where friends, family, and admirers from across the nation will gather to celebrate a life that inspired millions. In this time of sorrow, may we rededicate ourselves to the values Charlie held dear: faith, family, freedom, and fearless truth-telling. Our thoughts and prayers remain with Erika and the children, may God's peace surround them. == | Evil Will Not Win, The Movement Will Go On | | == | Erika Kirk delivered a moving and heartfelt message about her deep love for her remarkable husband, Charlie Kirk. He had the potential to be an extraordinary president of the United States one day. Tragically, Charlie's children will grow up without their father. Though this loss is profound, I trust that God will bring good from this sorrow, even in the midst of a difficult week. Charlie's unwavering commitment to loving Jesus Christ and upholding the founding principles of our nation will only grow stronger, inspiring countless others to carry his vision forward. My prayer is that his legacy and message will continue to draw our country closer to God, fostering a brighter future for all Americans. == | 5 Days Until Early Voting | | | Common Sense Over Nonsense | Team, Early voting begins in just 5 days, on September 19th! Let's spread the word to friends, family, and neighbors to vote Republican and support our shared values of God, Family, the Constitution, Freedom, a strong business climate, and common-sense policies. Recently, every House Democrat in Washington, D.C., voted against the Working Family Tax Cut Act, a bill that would have prevented the largest tax increase on the middle class in history, supported law and order, codified 28 Trump Executive Orders, promoted safe cities, and fueled economic growth. Instead, Democrats, led by figures like Spanberger, continue to push policies that defy reason and morality, including: Allowing boys in girls' bathrooms and sports Supporting gender surgeries for minors Advocating open borders Releasing dangerous criminals onto our streets * Defunding the police Our job as legislators is to be your voice and get things done. I've been able to pass bills each year where every republican and every democrat voted yes, but the policies supported by Spanberger stand in stark contrast to our commitment to law-abiding citizens and Christian values. We must reject the normalization of harmful ideas and ask: Why do Democrats prioritize criminals over hardworking families? Our team has been tirelessly knocking on doors, funding campaigns across Virginia, putting up signs, and greeting voters across the 5th Congressional District. All three statewide offices and all 100 House of Delegates seats are up for grabs this November. Winsome Sears and our full Republican ticket are aligned on the issues that matter, but victory depends on turning out the vote. For Freedom and Common Sense, I urge you to vote early on September 19th. This will free you up to help us rally others to the polls. Together, we can secure a brighter future for our district and our nation. Here are some photos from around the district and some of what we have been up to: | First White House Rose Garden Club Dinner | | Thank you President Trump for working tirelessly to Make America Great Again for all Americans! Fellow Veteran and Vice President JD Vance | | Speaker Mike Johnson | | | The Power of Prayer, Thank you Patricia! | | | Cumberland County | | It is an honor to meet folks around the 5th Congressional District. | Re-elect Delegate Chris Obenshain | | | Fellow Congressman Morgan Griffith and I attended an event to show support for Delegate Chris Obenshain's for re-election. Please remind family and friends early voting begins September 19. | Team McGuire Political Director Ramona Christian | | | A heartfelt thank you, to a very accomplished person, GOP Chair Ramona Christian for her unwavering dedication to Buckingham County, Virginia's 5th Congressional District, our Republican candidates, and President Trump. Her tireless America First leadership sets the example for other GOP chairs. Ramona and her teams efforts were instrumental in delivering record turnout for President Trump in 2024. From helping organize every Trump rally in Virginia to championing the entire Republican ticket, Ramona's commitment has never been about "me"; it's always been about "we." Ramona and her team's example is exactly what we need to secure victory this November. President Trump, Chris LaCivita, and the people of Virginia's 5th trust in Ramona Christian. For all things political for John McGuire in District 5, please send Ramona Christian an email to ramona@mcguireva.com | A Nice Birthday Present | | | Pastor Jonathan Falwell | | | | | Sen. Tim Kaine's recent remarks exposed a fundamental truth about the modern Democratic Party: they reject the principle that our inalienable rights are endowed by our Creator. The United States was founded on the belief that our rights, enshrined in the Constitution, are God-given and inherent to every individual. Yet, Kaine's comments suggest Democrats view these rights as privileges bestowed by the government. This is a profound and dangerous distinction. It reveals a worldview where rights are not sacred but subject to the whims of those in power. If Democrats believe the government grants our rights, they could just as easily revoke them upon regaining control. This perspective undermines the very foundation of our republic. Our Founding Fathers fought the Revolutionary War to reject tyranny and affirm that our liberties are not contingent on any ruler, regime, or political party. Our rights are not privileges to be dispensed or withdrawn by a dictator, monarch, or government. They are unalienable, endowed by God, and must be defended as such. | Thank you for your continued support. Together, we will take our country back. Please ask friends to vote early starting September 19. Take care, please tell a friend, and I look forward to seeing you around the district! == == == | For God and Country, | Congressman John McGuire VA 05 Soli Deo Gloria Jesus is my Savior Husband Father Navy SEAL Business Owner -Trump Endorsed -NRA Endorsed -Susan B. Anthony's List Endorsed -RJC Endorsed -Mark Levin Endorsed Pro-Life Pro-Gun Pro-Israel American Legion Member VFW (Life Member) NRA (Life Member) Highest Award for Conservatism (ACU/ CPAC) Family Foundation 100 VCDL 100 (Life Member) America First The Spirit of Virginia == our Facebook Page Twitter: @JohnMcGuire4VA Instagram: JohnMcGuire4VA == == john@mcguireva.com www.mcguireva.com | | | | == | | == | == | == == == | Paid for and Authorized by McGuire for Virginia | | | | McGuire for Virginia | PO Box 242 | Manakin Sabot, VA 23103 US | | | | | | |

name: Major General Paul Eaton, U.S. Army, retired
subject: Whatever they say the price tag is, double it
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
It seems leaders like Donald Trump, people who value perception over true force projection, can always find money for their vanity projects.
#
Peter, Paul Eaton here. Major General, U.S. Army, retired.
I’ve seen media coverage indicating initial estimates for the cost of Trump’s Department of War rebrand start at around $1 billion.
As someone with decades of service in the Army, I can give you some perspective on cost estimates like that: Whatever the first number is, double it.
It seems leaders like Donald Trump, people who value perception over true force projection, can always find money for their vanity projects. He can find hundreds of millions of dollars to throw himself a birthday party with 5000 Troops and dozens of tanks and helicopters, all at taxpayer expense. One million dollars a day to make National Guard Troops pick up trash on the National Mall in Washington. At least a billion dollars for this rebrand.
However, they can’t afford to hire desperately needed staff at the VA, where Veterans are regularly delayed (or, increasingly, denied) access to care.
But this is not up to Donald Trump. Per the Constitution, Congress still holds the power of the purse, and it would take an act of Congress to turn the Department of Defense into the Department of War. We can still have an impact on that process.
VoteVets is mobilizing our supporters and allies to stop this needless, wasteful rebrand. And to ensure we have the resources needed to defeat this, we’re humbly asking you:
[**Make a donation to VoteVets and help us stop the Department of War before it starts.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-sep25.qP0qt0&refcode=em250914-1-120&t=1&akid=34607%2E3013755%2EDwtIjI)

clean_body: Will you give $25 or even more so we can keep putting in the work to flip Virginia blue this November? = Peter, it looks like you haven't donated online to my campaign, so I'll cut right to the chase: I'm counting on you to join our donor list today. A few more donations from your area will help us stand up to Trump's attacks and continue sharing our agenda with Virginians before the mid-month fundraising deadline and the start of early voting later this week. = = = = = = = According to the polls, many Virginians have not yet decided who they'll support in this race. Some may see that as a challenge, but I see an incredible opportunity. As the Democratic nominee in Virginia, the only state where we can flip a governorship this year, I won't settle for a modest victory. I want to win big, and I want to set an example for Democrats in 2026, 2028, and beyond. But my opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has lined up far-right groups to foot the bill for attack ads across our Commonwealth. We have to respond right away to keep our own ads on the air and set the record straight. = = Thank you, Abigail Spanberger By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. Message & data rates may apply. Text HELP for more info. Text STOP to stop receiving messages. . Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Let me tell you what I said on MSNBC
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
I have a plan to get Virginia's economy back on track, and I'm ready to go anywhere and everywhere to talk about it.
Peter, I joined Lawrence O'Donnell on MSNBC to discuss my campaign for Governor of Virginia and the state of our economy, and I'd like to tell you about it.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94639?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu)
But first, as the Democratic nominee in the biggest election of Donald Trump's second term, I'm counting on grassroots donations to win big, to send a message to this White House, and to set an example for the entire nation.
**[So, will you contribute $10 or more today to our campaign and Heartland Patriots to help us contact undecided voters across Virginia? Early voting starts soon, and we need more donations to fund our organizing program.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94639?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu)**
If you have stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, we will process your contribution to Abigail Spanberger and Heartland Patriots instantly:
[Contribute $10](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94640?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=3&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu) [Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94640?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=4&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94640?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=5&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94640?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=6&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94640?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=7&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94640?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=8&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94639?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=9&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu)
---
Now, I didn't hold back in my interview with Lawrence, and I won't with you either.
**Donald Trump has devastated Virginia's economy, but my opponent won't stop cheering him on.**
Winsome Earle-Sears has said Trump's tax law does "so many great things."
The data is clear: 350,000 Virginians are projected to lose healthcare coverage and rural hospitals are set to close due to the so-called "Big, Beautiful Bill." Everyday goods are skyrocketing due to Trump's tariffs, and thousands of our civil servants are now unemployed due to DOGE.
_The Bureau of Labor Statistics just announced that Virginia lost more than 43,000 jobs earlier this year._ And our unemployment rate has ticked up for seven straight months. _(The last time that happened was the 2008 financial crisis.)_
Now, I have a plan to get Virginia's economy back on track, and I'm ready to go anywhere and everywhere to talk about it. Yes, that means more interviews, more events, and more TV ads.
But all this campaigning is expensive, and the donations from these emails go a _long way_ toward funding our work.
**[So, are you with me, Peter? I'm asking you to split $10 or even more today with Heartland Patriots to help us spread the word about our "Growing Virginia Plan" and stand up to Donald Trump's attacks.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/94639?refcode=em20250914-9409&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=9409%2E614464%2E6Siidu)**

name: Lombardo for Governor
subject: REMINDER: Join Governor Lombardo for a Special Announcement
body:
You don't want to miss this.
| | | | |
---
| REMINDER: Join Governor Lombardo for a Special Announcement
---
| Dear Nevada Supporters,
In case you missed our earlier email, we're excited to invite you to a Special Announcement with Governor Lombardo in both Las Vegas and Reno THIS upcoming Monday and Wednesday, and we need you to be part of these historic events!
Governor Lombardo has always answered the call to serve Nevada - first as Sheriff, now as our Governor - and always as a leader we can trust. As we begin this new chapter, we need you standing with us.
These events are the first step in building the movement to keep Nevada strong, safe, and prosperous, and your voice and support are critical.
Please RSVP below to ensure your spot and gain entry to the event!
LAS VEGAS
WHEN: Monday, September 15th Doors Open at 4 PM
WHERE: Rancho High School 1900 Searles Ave Las Vegas, NV 89101

# _Opinion:_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/45d43f42-c2db-444a-9e7f-7a074da51a1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**By PolitiBrawl Staff**
Olivia Rondeau is taking control of her story.
**[Rondeau](https://substack.com/redirect/f69c0fba-70d0-4291-af0e-fde035cd0be8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** is a reporter and commentator for **[Breitbart News](https://substack.com/redirect/7f67f6b2-8bdb-467c-adb1-f761a1a75e6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ,** covering agency and diplomatic beats across DC, with a history of features on **[FOX News](https://substack.com/redirect/79e5bd30-8800-4565-8c5e-6e0876d0cfde?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) , [The Daily Caller,](https://substack.com/redirect/caa6f156-4258-42b1-a2cc-da100b6baa7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **and numerous other publications.
She got her start as a brash conservative analyst back in high school, tweeting her "unpopular" political opinions and garnering over 10,000 followers at the age of 17, and from there landing journalist roles and primetime spots on national publications. Currently she has over a whopping 115,000 followers across [X](https://substack.com/redirect/f69c0fba-70d0-4291-af0e-fde035cd0be8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Youtube](https://substack.com/redirect/20e35fe8-17ee-46e2-9b35-76a70c83ad56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
According to Rondeau, her success expressing her opinion without apology or reverence to the confines of a political candidate or stereotype is due to one thing:
Her journey to becoming a decorated wrestler.
Eventually, Rondeau would go on to compete at the collegiate level as a 3x Girls State Champion and with a gold medal from the first female wrestling AAU Junior Olympic Games and first place trophy from the Super 32. The only thing stopping her wrestling momentum being the COVID-19 pandemic.
Behind all the accolades, though, is the politically-charged start of Rondeau's athletic career.
**A message from our sponsor (piece continues below)**

**[Together with Next Thing Technologies](https://substack.com/redirect/d744e901-f20b-4246-948e-8d363a86b5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **NVIDIA's $46.74B Earnings Prove AI Is Here to Stay ...And This Can Power It All**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d744e901-f20b-4246-948e-8d363a86b5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
NVIDIA shocked Wall Street again with **$46.74 billion in revenue** , up **56% year over year.**
Their CFO now expects**$3 -$4 trillion in AI infrastructure spending by 2030.**1
Oracle's results tell the same story.
The company reported**$14.9 billion in revenue, up 12% year over year.** Cloud infrastructure sales jumped**55%** , while long-term cloud contracts surged to**$455 billion -- a 359% increase in just one year.**
The message couldn't be clearer: **AI isn 't a bubble. It's the new economy.**1
**The Problem No One Talks About:**
Data centers and AI supercomputers run 24/7, devouring electricity.
But **lithium batteries can 't keep up**.1 They're too expensive, supplies are tight, and**China controls more than 80% of global lithium processing.**
If AI is going to scale to trillions of dollars, the real constraint isn't chips...
It's power storage.1
**The Breakthrough That Could Change Everything**
That's why **sodium-ion batteries** are emerging as the solution.1
* Sodium is**1,400x more abundant** **than lithium** , with significant reserves located in the U.S9
* Batteries can be made at a **fraction of the cost of lithium -- up to 90% less expensive in the first year of use.6**
* They last longer8, are safer7, and can be built at scale using domestic materials.1

And **Next Thing Technologies** , an American-owned company, is bringing this breakthrough to market. Their sodium-ion batteries are designed to power everything from **massive AI data centers** to **affordable home backup systems**.1
Think about it: NVIDIA builds the chips.
Oracle runs the cloud. But without safer, cheaper, U.S.-made batteries, the AI boom hits a wall.
Next Thing Technologies is building the infrastructure that could make it possible.1
Already, **7,800+ investors have committed more than $7 million. 3** And right now, [shares are available at just ](https://substack.com/redirect/d744e901-f20b-4246-948e-8d363a86b5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[$6 with up to 20% in bonus shares.4](https://substack.com/redirect/d744e901-f20b-4246-948e-8d363a86b5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[👉 ](https://substack.com/redirect/d744e901-f20b-4246-948e-8d363a86b5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[Click here to invest in the power behind the AI revolution.](https://substack.com/redirect/d744e901-f20b-4246-948e-8d363a86b5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* * *
**(Piece continues)**
Rondeau never imagined that in 2015 when she began wrestling at Poolesville High in Maryland, that she would ever come face to face with **male athletes.**
"I never really fathommed that I would be competing against boys when I joined."
"Thankfully, at my high school where I actually started wrestling, pretty much everyone was supportive of girls wrestling to the extent of like, if you're serious about it, then we're going to support you," Rondeau recounted, noting she wasn't the first girl to have ever joined, but was the only one at the time, and therefore had to face off against the high school men's teams.
Eventually, she would gain national attention for her success in these men's divisions, but the experience was not without some scathing scrutiny.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1d1ae31b-8d82-4356-a622-4f47e95e9951?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The wrestler described scenes of bullying and misogyny, a notable one being with a referee, who pointed at her before a match and said, "'And this is a boy sport, so you can't cry if you lose. You can't cry if you get hurt. So I don't want to hear it.'"
These moments, embedded throughout her years of training, only hardened Rondeau in a way that made her unafraid in her personal and professional endeavors, which she would need, as politics soon infiltrated her athletic life.
Real trouble began when Rondeau's success made her a symbol for transgender athlete advocates who used her wins as evidence against separating sport by gender for the sake of biological advantage.
Rondeau, a staunch advocate against males participating in female sports, reclaimed that narrative.
"I really don't like being used as a symbol for that cause," Rondeau said.
"It's really unfair to other women for a couple different reasons."
"I'm an anomaly...A boy in high school that weighs 106...He has not finished puberty yet. A girl at that age, she's smaller, but she can be a grown woman and be 106 and be cut and be strong and be stronger than that boy."
It was extremely rare, then, Rondeau explained, for a woman, whose biological weight distribution is categorically different from that of a man, to have success the way she did, and rightly so.
"I would always have, even at my most ripped in my life, _always_ have a higher fat percentage than a boy, my same weight, and that's necessary..." because women's bodies that are as cut as men's are, scientifically, "very unhealthy."
Besides the countless physical advantages of Rondeau's competitors, Rondeau also explained that the argument that wrestling itself is an exception because of its physicality, and that no contact sports like swimming, tennis, etc. are compatible with co-ed rules, was also misguided.
"That man has bigger lungs. He will always have a higher he would always be able to breathe in more breath and get more oxygen to his brain...His muscles will react faster than a woman's"
That female superstar? Rondeau assures "works on her technique 10 times more to get half as far as the boys." In fact, Rondeau herself would train year round in a relentless pursuit of success, compared to the male athletes she faced, who only participated in the sport seasonally.
Rondeau received national attention for competing successfully against men, with the Washington Post even writing an article about the high school superstar during her time on the male's team, and one would think, based on said attention, and the Hollywood-style arc of Rondeu's work ethic, that the result was a record of endless winning matches, with Rondeau slamming down her competitors in a symbolic beating of sexism.
However, that was far from the case.
"I wasn't ranked in the state against boys wrestling. I was good in my county. I was good at my level, but if I was a boy, nobody would have ever paid any attention to me..."
"...but I went on to become a multi-time national champion. I'm a six-time all-American in girls' wrestling. So, the fact that I'm not even ranked in the state in boys' wrestling, but I was the top girls wrestler in the country at one point in my weight class - that just goes to show the level of competition - how different it was."
"Any of the top county guys in Montgomery County, Maryland, where I'm from, or any of the private schools, really - anybody could have walked into a girls national tournament and placed all-American."
Rondeau isn't separate from the transgender community, either. In fact, a part of what makes her such a strong advocate for female sports, besides the fact that she is a female athlete, is that she is vocal and open with her community about her beliefs, fostering conversations that so many seem desperate to avoid (except when behind their screen shields).
"I actually have a couple transgender friends who really reject this... [they] would never compete against a woman or would ever want to make a woman uncomfortable."
Although feeling "vindicated" from the recent strides taken by the Trump administration to protect Title IX, from threatening universities' federal funds and fostering change in program rules, like earlier this year with **[UPenn's acquiescence to separate men and women sports](https://substack.com/redirect/18970e91-4cd6-4049-8fb0-11588fddfba0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg),** Rondeau says there's more to be done for female sports.
If anything, Rondeau believes the necessary change must come from the exact population this issue attacks: women.
"I think that women need to come together and really define what womanhood means before we can make meaningful change."
The journalist explained that, in all her conversations about the issue, it's seemingly always "affluent, liberal women" who, probably programmed as women to appease others, most ardently support trans athletes.
"I know I just called out misogyny...but I think...we kind of foster our own misogyny with that, because that is also a form of misogyny, thinking that womanhood is just an identity or a dress or you know a boob job or a surgery or something..."
The future of female sports rests, Rondeau said, on a deep cultural change, because an administration only lasts for a term, and as soon as it's gone, another could step in and force females back into athletic submission.
With all her accumulating obstacles not just on the mat, but in the stands, in press, and political circles, Rondeau had no choice but to harden against a world so set on boxing her up in a palatable societal role.
She credits the journey with sexism, physicality, thieves of identity and all, to her boldness expressing her opinions - often conservative, but not blindly so - to the world.
"I dealt with a lot of people selling me short and doubting my abilities. And so, that's why I just don't care."
And neither do those who inspire her, Rondeau pointed out, meeting countless female journalists and athletes since she began her journey: "They have their following. They built their base themselves. They don't care what people think."
They are out there, and they are fighting on the cultural mat, reclaiming their own story, and making all the difference for the future of women.

clean_body: A female wrestler who competed against men explains exactly why transgender athletes cannot be allowed to ruin fair competition for women... | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? A female wrestler who competed against men explains exactly why transgender athletes cannot be allowed to ruin fair competition for women... | | | Sep 14 | | | | | | | | | | | | Opinion: | | ---|---|--- By PolitiBrawl Staff Olivia Rondeau is taking control of her story. is a reporter and commentator for , covering agency and diplomatic beats across DC, with a history of features on , and numerous other publications. She got her start as a brash conservative analyst back in high school, tweeting her "unpopular" political opinions and garnering over 10,000 followers at the age of 17, and from there landing journalist roles and primetime spots on national publications. Currently she has over a whopping 115,000 followers across and . According to Rondeau, her success expressing her opinion without apology or reverence to the confines of a political candidate or stereotype is due to one thing: Her journey to becoming a decorated wrestler. Eventually, Rondeau would go on to compete at the collegiate level as a 3x Girls State Champion and with a gold medal from the first female wrestling AAU Junior Olympic Games and first place trophy from the Super 32. The only thing stopping her wrestling momentum being the COVID-19 pandemic. Behind all the accolades, though, is the politically-charged start of Rondeau's athletic career. A message from our sponsor (piece continues below) NVIDIA's $46.74B Earnings Prove AI Is Here to Stay ...And This Can Power It All | | ---|---|--- NVIDIA shocked Wall Street again with $46.74 billion in revenue , up 56% year over year. Their CFO now expects$3 -$4 trillion in AI infrastructure spending by 2030.1 Oracle's results tell the same story. The company reported$14.9 billion in revenue, up 12% year over year. Cloud infrastructure sales jumped55% , while long-term cloud contracts surged to$455 billion -- a 359% increase in just one year. The message couldn't be clearer: AI isn 't a bubble. It's the new economy.1 The Problem No One Talks About: Data centers and AI supercomputers run 24/7, devouring electricity. But lithium batteries can 't keep up.1 They're too expensive, supplies are tight, andChina controls more than 80% of global lithium processing. If AI is going to scale to trillions of dollars, the real constraint isn't chips... It's power storage.1 The Breakthrough That Could Change Everything That's why sodium-ion batteries are emerging as the solution.1 Sodium is1,400x more abundant than lithium , with significant reserves located in the U.S9 Batteries can be made at a fraction of the cost of lithium -- up to 90% less expensive in the first year of use.6 They last longer8, are safer7, and can be built at scale using domestic materials.1 And Next Thing Technologies , an American-owned company, is bringing this breakthrough to market. Their sodium-ion batteries are designed to power everything from massive AI data centers to affordable home backup systems.1 Think about it: NVIDIA builds the chips. Oracle runs the cloud. But without safer, cheaper, U.S.-made batteries, the AI boom hits a wall. Next Thing Technologies is building the infrastructure that could make it possible.1 Already, 7,800+ investors have committed more than $7 million. 3 And right now, (Piece continues) Rondeau never imagined that in 2015 when she began wrestling at Poolesville High in Maryland, that she would ever come face to face with male athletes. "I never really fathommed that I would be competing against boys when I joined." "Thankfully, at my high school where I actually started wrestling, pretty much everyone was supportive of girls wrestling to the extent of like, if you're serious about it, then we're going to support you," Rondeau recounted, noting she wasn't the first girl to have ever joined, but was the only one at the time, and therefore had to face off against the high school men's teams. Eventually, she would gain national attention for her success in these men's divisions, but the experience was not without some scathing scrutiny. | | ---|---|--- The wrestler described scenes of bullying and misogyny, a notable one being with a referee, who pointed at her before a match and said, "'And this is a boy sport, so you can't cry if you lose. You can't cry if you get hurt. So I don't want to hear it.'" These moments, embedded throughout her years of training, only hardened Rondeau in a way that made her unafraid in her personal and professional endeavors, which she would need, as politics soon infiltrated her athletic life. Real trouble began when Rondeau's success made her a symbol for transgender athlete advocates who used her wins as evidence against separating sport by gender for the sake of biological advantage. Rondeau, a staunch advocate against males participating in female sports, reclaimed that narrative. "I really don't like being used as a symbol for that cause," Rondeau said. "It's really unfair to other women for a couple different reasons." "I'm an anomaly...A boy in high school that weighs 106...He has not finished puberty yet. A girl at that age, she's smaller, but she can be a grown woman and be 106 and be cut and be strong and be stronger than that boy." It was extremely rare, then, Rondeau explained, for a woman, whose biological weight distribution is categorically different from that of a man, to have success the way she did, and rightly so. "I would always have, even at my most ripped in my life, always have a higher fat percentage than a boy, my same weight, and that's necessary..." because women's bodies that are as cut as men's are, scientifically, "very unhealthy." Besides the countless physical advantages of Rondeau's competitors, Rondeau also explained that the argument that wrestling itself is an exception because of its physicality, and that no contact sports like swimming, tennis, etc. are compatible with co-ed rules, was also misguided. "That man has bigger lungs. He will always have a higher he would always be able to breathe in more breath and get more oxygen to his brain...His muscles will react faster than a woman's" That female superstar? Rondeau assures "works on her technique 10 times more to get half as far as the boys." In fact, Rondeau herself would train year round in a relentless pursuit of success, compared to the male athletes she faced, who only participated in the sport seasonally. Rondeau received national attention for competing successfully against men, with the Washington Post even writing an article about the high school superstar during her time on the male's team, and one would think, based on said attention, and the Hollywood-style arc of Rondeu's work ethic, that the result was a record of endless winning matches, with Rondeau slamming down her competitors in a symbolic beating of sexism. However, that was far from the case. "I wasn't ranked in the state against boys wrestling. I was good in my county. I was good at my level, but if I was a boy, nobody would have ever paid any attention to me..." "...but I went on to become a multi-time national champion. I'm a six-time all-American in girls' wrestling. So, the fact that I'm not even ranked in the state in boys' wrestling, but I was the top girls wrestler in the country at one point in my weight class - that just goes to show the level of competition - how different it was." "Any of the top county guys in Montgomery County, Maryland, where I'm from, or any of the private schools, really - anybody could have walked into a girls national tournament and placed all-American." Rondeau isn't separate from the transgender community, either. In fact, a part of what makes her such a strong advocate for female sports, besides the fact that she is a female athlete, is that she is vocal and open with her community about her beliefs, fostering conversations that so many seem desperate to avoid (except when behind their screen shields). "I actually have a couple transgender friends who really reject this... [they] would never compete against a woman or would ever want to make a woman uncomfortable." Although feeling "vindicated" from the recent strides taken by the Trump administration to protect Title IX, from threatening universities' federal funds and fostering change in program rules, like earlier this year with , Rondeau says there's more to be done for female sports. If anything, Rondeau believes the necessary change must come from the exact population this issue attacks: women. "I think that women need to come together and really define what womanhood means before we can make meaningful change." The journalist explained that, in all her conversations about the issue, it's seemingly always "affluent, liberal women" who, probably programmed as women to appease others, most ardently support trans athletes. "I know I just called out misogyny...but I think...we kind of foster our own misogyny with that, because that is also a form of misogyny, thinking that womanhood is just an identity or a dress or you know a boob job or a surgery or something..." The future of female sports rests, Rondeau said, on a deep cultural change, because an administration only lasts for a term, and as soon as it's gone, another could step in and force females back into athletic submission. With all her accumulating obstacles not just on the mat, but in the stands, in press, and political circles, Rondeau had no choice but to harden against a world so set on boxing her up in a palatable societal role. She credits the journey with sexism, physicality, thieves of identity and all, to her boldness expressing her opinions - often conservative, but not blindly so - to the world. "I dealt with a lot of people selling me short and doubting my abilities. And so, that's why I just don't care." And neither do those who inspire her, Rondeau pointed out, meeting countless female journalists and athletes since she began her journey: "They have their following. They built their base themselves. They don't care what people think." They are out there, and they are fighting on the cultural mat, reclaiming their own story, and making all the difference for the future of women. name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: I'll be direct, folks.
body:
|
#
I'll be direct, folks. With our end-of-quarter deadline fast approaching, I'm going to ask you to make a $3 contribution to help us meet our goal. But before I do, I want to take a moment and reflect on how far we’ve come together. **The truth is, on paper, the district I represent here in Michigan shouldn’t be held by a Democrat. It went for Trump by 2 points in the same election I won this race.** But I know our community, and I believed that if we ran a positive campaign, and stayed laser-focused on the true challenges working families face, we'd build a broad, winning coalition to support our vision. **Your support proved me right.** Together, we defied the odds and showed that when you focus on what matters and truly listen, people come together. And with this deadline closing in, we can’t take our foot off the gas. The same folks who said this seat was out of reach are now flooding our district with lying attack ads against me -- more than in any other House race in the country. They’re hoping we stumble and slow down when it matters most. This deadline is our chance to prove them wrong: [**So what do you say, friend? Will you step up with an urgent $3 donation to help us show the strength of our grassroots movement? Dear Fellow American, A fifth-grade girl traveled with her Colorado school on a field trip across the country.

You can probably imagine how she must have felt in the days and weeks leading up to the trip. Excited to spend a few days with her friends. Eager to visit a new place. And, sure, a bit nervous--it's a big deal to stay overnight in a place you've never been before, especially as an 11-year-old.

**Now imagine how she felt when she found out --already hundreds of miles from home and in her hotel room for the night--_that she was supposed to share a bed with a boy._**

Shocked. Confused. Uncomfortable.

She slipped into the bathroom to call her mom, who was thankfully on the trip with her. Her mom thought she must have misunderstood. She couldn't fathom that the school assigned her daughter to room overnight with a boy.

But it wasn't a misunderstanding. It was _ school policy_.

The policy says that students on overnight trips will be assigned rooms based on their "gender identity," not on biology. And the school refuses to notify parents or students of the policy, or if a student of the opposite sex is assigned to the room.

That means a girl can be assigned to share a hotel room with a boy who identifies as a girl, without mom and dad's--or the student's--knowledge or say.

**How would you feel if this happened to your child or grandchild?**

It shouldn't have to be said--but this is just **WRONG.**

It's common sense that girls and boys deserve to have their own private spaces. And if that privacy could be infringed upon, parents need to know so that they can make informed decisions for their children.

Families across the country are being harmed by policies like these. That's why the Waileses, along with three other Colorado families, are pushing back in a lawsuit--for their kids and many others like them.

**Please raise your voice to say NO MORE to policies that deny truth and violate parents ' rights--and to protect kids from harmful policies that put gender ideology above their comfort and privacy.** [ SIGN THE STATEMENT](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/lrmyvFiYGZb2ZIsfagdvhdo0gW3yC43bcotD6T8x_2Rar3xXWWcD4qfJyTGXXnmJ_DZ0zEaGmDMYzih3L_LmObYva8c0xi7hRUPeNF5DAoQLJEO0j0CUGwP56-p-Ne2pI40UjxAfFRE63Z6dDA_W8NSrgeSIuCcUmKxDAIh_9xM66EZ4k1BWiG0RPLtI7EJ5nthi3_4-u_FBLgHtr04r6oUUz54WV4cWd_TzRRSZQfWwjjFmA8FZNqQ2S6xb0Jq3D8sLJS84sw0HjF-3M8A1vJK4GHRDdKrdAjGwLbLJv6PpJxfKCx5XmlJ4zTzkbk9v8g2qAFYEBt-OZudPB0pqdJydBTx5BehB6Brof-djUt0fQR8s4kU7WKpz-1gadyz7_8k_KTdUI1jtpO-tIjWxLQ7cdvHRCLHuxGraAt79IsbiQ3tI4rvcGuvSInTFy9VYZ9dauVkiKZVX2XTnIOP02pRpnoz-u3HloYrakJSCih9Crt40DeZW5r5mHmLKotWR7nMtRH860tlYcL1ZnQCW93g6IMFp_jtR1Bjuzua6i1H0sfbHNJ0Zms4HilwAe7GTNPg_E8oszGGnIVv2IBXbwEjnojtIpGo78urR0bkS5nAAeBshbWoHwhHumLdWDpy8WxAdgmqlDgPRj6rDShB6rqgWjryr4Dkm4-2W4nHYJY52moAX01a5Ro_t1f9JpJDodEy2aiyOGxRKwXI/RqKLhWqG9FWg-2KblqZB8pb-JpG6GaYV/12)

Legal Counsel, Center for Parental Rights

Colorado parents are pushing back after the school district assigned their daughter to share a room with a boy. Alliance Defending Freedom 15100 N. 90th Street, Scottsdale, Arizona 85260

name: James Talarico HQ
subject: pummeled
body:
[1]Talarico for Texas

Peter — Ken Paxton just personally
attacked James.

He called James a “lunatic” and accused him of “distorting Christianity.”

And this is just the opening attack. Our opponents will say whatever it
takes to smear James.

[ https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em250914-ak&t=1001&akid=37%2E1347274%2ECV1uNr ]Please make a $5 contribution right now to make sure we
have the resources to fight back. We won’t let these attacks go
unanswered.

Look, politics is rough. Especially in Texas.

James knew these attacks were coming, but it’s also very telling.

It’s been less than a week, and they’re already scared.

They see the movement we’re building. They see the demand from Texans for
new leaders who will be a voice for the people — not corporate interests
and billionaire donors.

Our opponents are going to lie. And they are going to put millions of
dollars behind their lies and baseless attacks.

They’re going to pummel James on every Texans’ screen.

name: Chris Pappas
subject: We’re so close to hitting our goal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
We have until midnight tomorrow to hit our goal.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=2344%2E582294%2Ew1iPaD)
Time is running out:
**Our campaign has until midnight tomorrow to reach our mid-month goal of hitting 20,000 individual donors — and we’re only a few thousand away.**
We’ve come too close to reaching this goal to fall short in the last 48 hours — and with a new poll showing this race tighter than ever, we must demonstrate the strength of our campaign.
Reaching 20,000 individual donors proves the strength of the grassroots movement powering this campaign.
It could also mean the difference between winning and losing in one of the most competitive races in the country, where Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans are going to do whatever it takes to flip this seat red.
When I ran for Congress, I promised to put New Hampshire first, work with anyone to get results, and always stand up for our state’s values. I’m proud of my record and for sticking by my word, and I’ll do the same in the Senate. But first we have to win this competitive race.
If everyone who reads this pitches in today, even just a little, we will hit our goal and stay on track to win this race. [**Will you donate $5 or whatever you can before Monday night?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250914_1_mm?refcode=em20250914-2344&amount=5&refcode2=2344_582294_w1iPaD&akid=2344%2E582294%2Ew1iPaD)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250914_1_mm?amount=5&refcode=em20250914-2344&refcode2=2344_582294_w1iPaD&akid=2344%2E582294%2Ew1iPaD)**
Your support means the world to me. Thanks for joining me in this movement.
With gratitude,
Chris Pappas
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer&refcode=em20250914-1?t=2&refcode2=2344_582294_w1iPaD&akid=2344%2E582294%2Ew1iPaD)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=3&akid=2344%2E582294%2Ew1iPaD)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=2344%2E582294%2Ew1iPaD)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)

body:

We need your signature to grow our grassroots effort to fight back »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115201010/569521798/399364649?refcode=NDCAF_20250914_EMPET_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMTBmZDAxMC03YjkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RXj6SiZzMuh4GeIosPzpQ3kdT9JWRTfDjUC156vIjug=&emci=f045499d-928f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=110fd010-7b91-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=48524772)** | [SIGN NOW:ADD YOUR NAME TO DEFEND MAIL-IN VOTING »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115201012/569521799/399364649?refcode=NDCAF_20250914_EMPET_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMTBmZDAxMC03YjkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RXj6SiZzMuh4GeIosPzpQ3kdT9JWRTfDjUC156vIjug=&emci=f045499d-928f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=110fd010-7b91-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=48524772)
---
Earlier this week, Donald Trump announced that he will _lead_ the movement to end mail-in-voting!

**He’s pushing to eliminate mail-in ballots and voting machines,** claiming — _without providing a shred of evidence_ — that they cause voter fraud.

**Not only are his claims false, they’re dangerous.** This narrative is a key part of Trump and MAGA Republicans’ “Big Lie” conspiracy that Democrats “stole” the 2020 election.

New Dems are already fighting back in Congress because we know how critical mail-in voting is to keeping elections accessible — especially for seniors, rural communities, and Americans with disabilities.

clean_body: Add your signature to help fight back >> | BREAKING: Donald Trump is drafting an executive order to ban mail-in voting! » | | Earlier this week, Donald Trump announced that he will lead the movement to end mail-in-voting! He’s pushing to eliminate mail-in ballots and voting machines, claiming — without providing a shred of evidence — that they cause voter fraud. Not only are his claims false, they’re dangerous. This narrative is a key part of Trump and MAGA Republicans’ “Big Lie” conspiracy that Democrats “stole” the 2020 election. New Dems are already fighting back in Congress because we know how critical mail-in voting is to keeping elections accessible — especially for seniors, rural communities, and Americans with disabilities. Let’s get it done!
