* * *
* * *
The news rippled through Washington and Wall Street like a seismic wave. Traders paused, policymakers scrambled, and ordinary Americans wondered: If the data we rely on is this unreliable, what else might we be missing? The revision not only recast the narrative of the past year's labor market but also ignited debates about institutional competence, political interference, and the Federal Reserve's stubborn stance on interest rates. At its core, this episode raises a profound question: In an era of hyper-partisan scrutiny, can we still trust the numbers that shape our economic reality?
**[@Geiger_capitol said it best:](https://substack.com/redirect/cd61df05-3285-4e27-95f9-1a1a4bcdf993?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/cd61df05-3285-4e27-95f9-1a1a4bcdf993?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The mechanics of the BLS revision are rooted in a routine but opaque process. Each year, the agency benchmarks its monthly employment surveys against more comprehensive data from unemployment insurance records, which cover nearly the entire workforce. This year's preliminary adjustment, released on September 9, 2025, showed that job growth averaged just 71,000 per month--half the originally reported 147,000. According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics' official release , the downward shift affected sectors like leisure and hospitality, which had been overestimated amid lingering pandemic disruptions.
Historically, such revisions are not uncommon; they averaged around 100,000 jobs in either direction over the past decade. But this one's magnitude stands out. **[As reported by The New York Times,](https://substack.com/redirect/6e87a07c-703a-473d-9e88-5719c46b46aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** it surpasses the 818,000 downward adjustment for the prior year and the record 902,000 drop in 2009, during the Great Recession. Economists attribute the discrepancy to challenges in data collection--small businesses underreporting, seasonal adjustments gone awry, and the lingering effects of remote work blurring traditional metrics. Yet, in a polarized climate, explanations veer toward suspicion.
Vice President JD Vance criticized the BLS for incompetence and lambasted the agency as "useless" a sentiment echoed in White House statements following the latest data release.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1c6fbb1b-12bf-45c5-8f4b-a5e85598784d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
President Trump, never one to shy from controversy, had previously fired BLS Commissioner Erika McEntarfer in August before the latest revision, citing "incompetence" rather than the numbers themselves and the White House released a statement documenting a **["lengthy history of inaccuracies."](https://substack.com/redirect/869dad70-ede4-4342-b025-e9b411d5e418?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Critics decried the firing then as an assault on institutional independence, reminiscent of Trump's past pressures on federal agencies. However with the latest revision it now appears somewhat prescient.
The fallout extended to monetary policy too, where Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell found himself once again in the crosshairs. Throughout 2025, Powell had resisted rate cuts, preaching caution while citing lurking risks of "tariff based inflation" from the new administration's trade policy and even going so far as resisting cuts as the job market visibly weakened in July and August of 2025.
But the revised jobs data now reveal a much weaker economy, suggesting his caution was misplaced and indeed may have prolonged unnecessary hardship. **[As Yahoo Finance noted,](https://substack.com/redirect/b53e229a-068b-48d4-8c5f-d204469e8153?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Trump lambasted Powell as a "total disaster" for being "too late" on cuts, intensifying demands for accountability. Indeed, with job growth now revealed as anemic, Powell's decisions--holding rates steady amid what appeared to be robust employment--look increasingly misguided. **[Reuters reported that](https://substack.com/redirect/dd8f7bec-118d-4e08-90c5-27678830ddf9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the revision implies the labor market was "weaker than thought," potentially forcing the Fed to recalibrate.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/89299a33-f812-4845-b4dd-b8fd324f3ee0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In a previous op-ed we at The Capitalist made a none to veiled assertion that Jerome Powell's actions regarding interest rates appeared to be more guided by politics than financial conditions**.**
## [Is politics why Powell won't cut rates?](https://substack.com/redirect/63f0bc17-071f-4a82-bb83-d7537f2c16a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [The Capitalist](https://substack.com/profile/333697867-the-capitalist)| | *
---|---|---
| Aug 7
[](https://substack.com/redirect/63f0bc17-071f-4a82-bb83-d7537f2c16a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[In the intricate dance of monetary policy, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has positioned himself as a cautious maestro, orchestrating a delicate balance between inflation and employment.](https://substack.com/redirect/63f0bc17-071f-4a82-bb83-d7537f2c16a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/63f0bc17-071f-4a82-bb83-d7537f2c16a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Given what we now know about how wildly inaccurate the job numbers were that he has been using to justify his actions, his implacable stance on interest rate cuts since Trump got elected at best makes him look incompetent. At worst it makes him look either willfully political or actively pursuing a policy that has harmed Americans and the American Economy. None of which are going to quieten the calls for his resignation.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/573c5ac3-880c-406c-84c2-df875b2b4bfe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
No amount of pleading caution over "potential tariff based inflation" is going to get him off the hook this time. At this point there should be a Congressional hearing on what Powell knew and when he knew it. Because either he didn't know the numbers were so wildly skewed (which is concerning to say the least. - One would hope that the Fed Chair has an accurate number of people in work given that their mission is literally to support that.) Or he knew that the numbers were fake and pursued his policy anyway, which opens the lid on a whole Pandora's box of very awkward questions.

It has sadly become apparent from what has been uncovered so far that the previous Biden administration certainly had a "relaxed" relationship with "the truth." It was not above sliding things through departments without going through the proper channels (John Brennan and the Crossfire Hurricane dossier) or just making up things from scratch (the Steele dossier.)
Given the supporting proof of both of those instances, is it really a stretch to wonder if employment numbers, over time, maybe, got a little "juiced?" Would you get away with it? Would anybody notice?
This isn't merely about numbers; it's about the erosion of public faith. When data swings this wildly, it undermines confidence in everything from stock markets to personal financial planning. Polls already show declining trust in government statistics, with a 2023 Pew survey indicating only 37% of Americans believe economic data is accurate. In this vacuum, conspiracy theories flourish.
Most concerning of all however is that interest rate decisions broadly speaking are made by balancing the labor market on one side of the scale and inflation on the other. If for the last year nearly a million jobs on that scale have turned out to be fake, that would likely have had an effect on if rates would have been cut sooner.
Would debt levels be as high as they are now if rates had been lower? Would the housing market be in a better shape now if rates had been lower? Would credit card delinquencies be so high now if rates had been lower? All of these questions (and countless others) are affected by what rate the Fed sets. If a major component part of that decision making process is flawed, then the decisions stemming from it are likely flawed too.
The American public is greatly affected by the decisions the Fed makes. It is the duty of The Fed to make the best decisions possible guided not by political bias or fake data.
Experts argue these revisions stem from methodological flaws, not malice. This maybe true, as Hanlon's Razor states _**"Never attribute to malice that which is adequately explained by stupidity " **_but a million fake jobs strains credulity.
Certainly reforms to the methodological process like better real-time tracking via digital tools and the current push to put government data on like this on the blockchain to increase transparency would make a difference and prevent such huge revisions in the future.
Regardless of how we move forward, this massive revision compels a reckoning. If economic data is the compass guiding policy, its unreliability risks steering us astray. In complex systems, certainty is always illusory but by embracing uncertainty and investing in better metrics, we might rebuild the trust that sustains a healthy democracy. Congress should heed the calls for hearings on the BLS and the Fed to begin to reclaim that trust.
After all, phantom jobs may vanish, but the consequences linger.

It was the largest such revision in over a decade, evoking memories of the 2009 financial crisis when similar corrections exposed deep hitherto unforeseen vulnerabilities. | | ---|---|--- NVIDIA shocked Wall Street again with $46.74 billion in revenue , up 56% year over year. Their CFO now expects$3 -$4 trillion in AI infrastructure spending by 2030.1 Oracle's results tell the same story. The company reported$14.9 billion in revenue, up 12% year over year. Cloud infrastructure sales jumped55% , while long-term cloud contracts surged to$455 billion -- a 359% increase in just one year. The message couldn't be clearer: AI isn 't a bubble. It's the new economy.1 The Problem No One Talks About: Data centers and AI supercomputers run 24/7, devouring electricity. But lithium batteries can 't keep up.1 They're too expensive, supplies are tight, andChina controls more than 80% of global lithium processing. If AI is going to scale to trillions of dollars, the real constraint isn't chips... It's power storage.1 The Breakthrough That Could Change Everything That's why sodium-ion batteries are emerging as the solution.1 Sodium is1,400x more abundant than lithium , with significant reserves located in the U.S9 Batteries can be made at a fraction of the cost of lithium -- up to 90% less expensive in the first year of use.6 They last longer8, are safer7, and can be built at scale using domestic materials.1 And Next Thing Technologies , an American-owned company, is bringing this breakthrough to market. Their sodium-ion batteries are designed to power everything from massive AI data centers to affordable home backup systems.1 Think about it: NVIDIA builds the chips. Oracle runs the cloud. But without safer, cheaper, U.S.-made batteries, the AI boom hits a wall. Next Thing Technologies is building the infrastructure that could make it possible.1 Already, 7,800+ investors have committed more than $7 million. 3 And right now, The news rippled through Washington and Wall Street like a seismic wave. Traders paused, policymakers scrambled, and ordinary Americans wondered: If the data we rely on is this unreliable, what else might we be missing? The revision not only recast the narrative of the past year's labor market but also ignited debates about institutional competence, political interference, and the Federal Reserve's stubborn stance on interest rates. At its core, this episode raises a profound question: In an era of hyper-partisan scrutiny, can we still trust the numbers that shape our economic reality? | | ---|---|--- The mechanics of the BLS revision are rooted in a routine but opaque process. Each year, the agency benchmarks its monthly employment surveys against more comprehensive data from unemployment insurance records, which cover nearly the entire workforce. This year's preliminary adjustment, released on September 9, 2025, showed that job growth averaged just 71,000 per month--half the originally reported 147,000. According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics' official release , the downward shift affected sectors like leisure and hospitality, which had been overestimated amid lingering pandemic disruptions. Historically, such revisions are not uncommon; they averaged around 100,000 jobs in either direction over the past decade. But this one's magnitude stands out. it surpasses the 818,000 downward adjustment for the prior year and the record 902,000 drop in 2009, during the Great Recession. Economists attribute the discrepancy to challenges in data collection--small businesses underreporting, seasonal adjustments gone awry, and the lingering effects of remote work blurring traditional metrics. Yet, in a polarized climate, explanations veer toward suspicion. Vice President JD Vance criticized the BLS for incompetence and lambasted the agency as "useless" a sentiment echoed in White House statements following the latest data release. | | ---|---|--- President Trump, never one to shy from controversy, had previously fired BLS Commissioner Erika McEntarfer in August before the latest revision, citing "incompetence" rather than the numbers themselves and the White House released a statement documenting a Critics decried the firing then as an assault on institutional independence, reminiscent of Trump's past pressures on federal agencies. However with the latest revision it now appears somewhat prescient. The fallout extended to monetary policy too, where Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell found himself once again in the crosshairs. Throughout 2025, Powell had resisted rate cuts, preaching caution while citing lurking risks of "tariff based inflation" from the new administration's trade policy and even going so far as resisting cuts as the job market visibly weakened in July and August of 2025. But the revised jobs data now reveal a much weaker economy, suggesting his caution was misplaced and indeed may have prolonged unnecessary hardship. Trump lambasted Powell as a "total disaster" for being "too late" on cuts, intensifying demands for accountability. Indeed, with job growth now revealed as anemic, Powell's decisions--holding rates steady amid what appeared to be robust employment--look increasingly misguided. the revision implies the labor market was "weaker than thought," potentially forcing the Fed to recalibrate. | | ---|---|--- In a previous op-ed we at The Capitalist made a none to veiled assertion that Jerome Powell's actions regarding interest rates appeared to be more guided by politics than financial conditions. | | | | ---|---|--- | Aug 7 | Given what we now know about how wildly inaccurate the job numbers were that he has been using to justify his actions, his implacable stance on interest rate cuts since Trump got elected at best makes him look incompetent. At worst it makes him look either willfully political or actively pursuing a policy that has harmed Americans and the American Economy. None of which are going to quieten the calls for his resignation. | | ---|---|--- No amount of pleading caution over "potential tariff based inflation" is going to get him off the hook this time. At this point there should be a Congressional hearing on what Powell knew and when he knew it. Because either he didn't know the numbers were so wildly skewed (which is concerning to say the least. - One would hope that the Fed Chair has an accurate number of people in work given that their mission is literally to support that.) Or he knew that the numbers were fake and pursued his policy anyway, which opens the lid on a whole Pandora's box of very awkward questions. It has sadly become apparent from what has been uncovered so far that the previous Biden administration certainly had a "relaxed" relationship with "the truth." It was not above sliding things through departments without going through the proper channels (John Brennan and the Crossfire Hurricane dossier) or just making up things from scratch (the Steele dossier.) Given the supporting proof of both of those instances, is it really a stretch to wonder if employment numbers, over time, maybe, got a little "juiced?" Would you get away with it? Would anybody notice? This isn't merely about numbers; it's about the erosion of public faith. When data swings this wildly, it undermines confidence in everything from stock markets to personal financial planning. Polls already show declining trust in government statistics, with a 2023 Pew survey indicating only 37% of Americans believe economic data is accurate. In this vacuum, conspiracy theories flourish. Most concerning of all however is that interest rate decisions broadly speaking are made by balancing the labor market on one side of the scale and inflation on the other. If for the last year nearly a million jobs on that scale have turned out to be fake, that would likely have had an effect on if rates would have been cut sooner. Would debt levels be as high as they are now if rates had been lower? Would the housing market be in a better shape now if rates had been lower? Would credit card delinquencies be so high now if rates had been lower? All of these questions (and countless others) are affected by what rate the Fed sets. If a major component part of that decision making process is flawed, then the decisions stemming from it are likely flawed too. The American public is greatly affected by the decisions the Fed makes. It is the duty of The Fed to make the best decisions possible guided not by political bias or fake data. Experts argue these revisions stem from methodological flaws, not malice. This maybe true, as Hanlon's Razor states "Never attribute to malice that which is adequately explained by stupidity " but a million fake jobs strains credulity. Certainly reforms to the methodological process like better real-time tracking via digital tools and the current push to put government data on like this on the blockchain to increase transparency would make a difference and prevent such huge revisions in the future. Regardless of how we move forward, this massive revision compels a reckoning. If economic data is the compass guiding policy, its unreliability risks steering us astray. In complex systems, certainty is always illusory but by embracing uncertainty and investing in better metrics, we might rebuild the trust that sustains a healthy democracy. Congress should heed the calls for hearings on the BLS and the Fed to begin to reclaim that trust. After all, phantom jobs may vanish, but the consequences linger. clean_body: Early voting starts on Friday, so every donation matters. = Two days, Peter. We have just two days until the biggest mid-month deadline of the campaign so far, and we're at risk of missing our critical donor goal. Your contribution today — whatever the amount — will pay for the ads, organizers, yard signs, and other investments that convince undecided voters to support Abigail Spanberger. Early voting starts on Friday, so every donation matters. = = = = = = = = Thank you, Team Spanberger By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. Message & data rates may apply. Text HELP for more info. Text STOP to stop receiving messages. . Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. If the Secret Service isn’t yet investigating Governor Tim Walz, they ought to be.

He and his wife are known for saying some crazy things since they came onto the national stage last summer, but **this takes the cake.**

We’ve been warning everyone for years that the crazy would set in and something terrible would happen, and last summer, _**we came very close to losing the Commander-in-Chief, but by the grace of God, the bullet missed. **_

It was only a matter of time until a sitting U.S. governor uttered the words, but _**now it’s happened, and there is no turning back.**_

name: Deb Haaland HQ
subject: Important update from Deb for New Mexico
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Can we count on you?
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=4420%2E400544%2EnixvrQ)
We’re writing with an important update.
**Our next state fundraising deadline is October 6 — after which, Deb is legally required to publicly report how much we’ve raised and how many donations we’ve received for the last time before the election year.**
Deb doesn’t come from wealth, and she’s never had the means to self-fund a campaign.
**That means National Republicans and potential opponents will be looking at our numbers to see if we have the massive grassroots support we’ll need to compete and win as we head into 2026.**
And honestly, right now we don’t.
MAGA forces have already set their eyes on New Mexico, and if they see any sign of weakness, they’ll go _all in_ to defeat us and flip our state red.
To help us show the strength of our campaign, we set a goal to bring in **10,000 grassroots donations** before midnight tomorrow – but that means we need the support of every supporter, including you.
**[Can we count on you to help hit our September mid-month goal? A gift of $5 — or any amount — today will send a powerful message in our report and help us to build the momentum we need to win this race.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250914_1_mm?amount=5&refcode=em20250914-4420&refcode2=4420_400544_nixvrQ&akid=4420%2E400544%2EnixvrQ)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250914_1_mm?amount=5&refcode=em20250914-4420&refcode2=4420_400544_nixvrQ&akid=4420%2E400544%2EnixvrQ)**
Thank you!
Team Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250914-1&t=2&refcode2=4420_400544_nixvrQ&akid=4420%2E400544%2EnixvrQ)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Sign up for Texts](https://deb4gov.us/text-me)
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=4420%2E400544%2EnixvrQ)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=4420%2E400544%2EnixvrQ)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)
PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO

name: Suzanne Beecher
subject: Help stop gender ideology from destroying fairness for female athletes.
body:
Women shouldn’t have to share locker rooms with men.

| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/u/sFrn0BJzAktqMD0pXi6Fn3F1jVnwKL27zBvmJTFW5nSVAwWqXTUqfancxNyh-rlMe5RZnGU2xXbdmzKDZOAyYlgon84dCka9ovOE23X41hfJxtY71zOFYmeDL2nWOjOk6V3QOLAB9zZ4H-6sNHKTHY2lYS22Ixoy2kLeEyBaGl7r8c4PZItPo3_o/FPgBGgBB0rib3w2VAGV2i7EKvRVHmloT/12)_

**** Dear Defender of Truth, Women shouldn't have to share locker rooms with men. They shouldn't have to compete against male athletes. And they shouldn't be punished for speaking the truth.

But across America, that's exactly what's happening.

Men are being allowed to compete in women's sports--robbing girls of scholarships, medals, titles, and safety.

Now, for the first time in history, the U.S. Supreme Court has agreed to hear two cases--_State of West Virginia v. B.P.J._ and _Little v. Hecox_ --that could decide the future of women's sports nationwide.

This could be a watershed moment in the fight to protect biological reality and fairness.

If you believe women's sports should be reserved for women, we need your voice today.

[Sign the Declaration on Women’s Sports »](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/Iqw3gqdRSTX1ckS5GOi0x_NE8Tvpnus7_Yyaydfs94VnDUd8ZvhKHCVjBiTo0DTf0tMssznaG9hNYFMvBLvb4EFwbMoGWLA81vtveJGudSPLGy5rn9GJt9afiqax8LlTJX1yM80IukfuoNDaCJ3GGRf_NMN8OsReZPjAwrLYItiT_oxVY7c4kGc01C0bciUKLeomkGQX-puxv_1qWDMAEJFzUqNJ53Xllp53BL8rnCIWtMo0Fpu1OuEPhUQA0hh9PIVWJmadCEU5H_j6NMphgOGgOaOyaxn1jjn5byMk7VgbGP3mFeSTGLyf518yrUbg8pD0X3aN212ukAb5J3GXoM_2aR92eWcAi2yj1iMs-_2LEhN2nfni6ThOG6W0DxOfp_VvTcrxP74gV8SoeS7lUEeIVsO6YOXC_tx5bnqJrJXymcsiVgNHJq2p5nC5le_vjwZZA-2jy8J6YQdL9ks1kGSUhNtJ-SQzWzn4tCQ1kH1CVWTy4cg9FP5IzslNlb2DdX64Qoqm1oxq4KeB-vq7F5o3uk79IQTQs5o-h4Ty6xQ1BbsijkdyRhYQPKRpDiYBPGjLzbnhW_58NYgeOPBgxDuiJaIV_EJjyZ22GiNiGRzLbpo28JKqBz33rqDq_qL4ipMAUhxKFtyrVJbZziX51NSv8Kcq0LSS05MLOXcMZvudIpUg2wJqkyjU4zENq9xL0wjb0B0W4ONrnNqBKzdxbfy-doybyzYyHvUtipcn_sM0vnjYKtzATzv4qT_M/QgjSeodlucPAshVggkCTaCSm42C8QH8g/12)

Your signature will say clearly:
✅ Men should not be allowed to compete on women's teams
✅ Women deserve privacy, fairness, and safety
✅ Radical gender ideology must not ignore biological truth

After you sign, you'll be taken to the next critical step...

Make a gift to support female athletes like Lainey Armistead, a courageous former college soccer player who is helping defend her state's Save Women's Sports Act in court.

Your gift of any amount will help Alliance Defending Freedom stand strong at the Supreme Court--at no cost to our clients.

🚨 We've won 16 cases at the Supreme Court since 2011. With your help, and God's blessing, we can win again.

Please add your name now--and then take the next bold step to protect women's sports with a generous gift.

[Add your name and make your gift »](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/HrXZo8G6u4c_W6pmV4SuYgt6u-kiSuTwzywotzBcxPM3mlrvuXbE8kE1wKykC9T8SLvKCbClh0aiUHqlcAIO6coDJ5-fUbXPCuYg0jjDgLOUjk-7r4R8w8yQVNgc48SQ8YpTbGmgAjz5PX0y23GvoRdA5kRvnZA0ridItmmReEVPdy9pZtynsOe_klJS7bgec3lFhgH1o9anbBYmPAAZ1qlWmYAMnJqZlu8gfQOUu799TjFTPcIQk_wV4Cf1lmgKadUoUUhFNL8EvXarGoO163El6Bg2aFuZ3w4pRME-UQ67tJRsHtsSfyoViZociX2iF0yAvKnFX_E_jjQVdlAYgkcbnovkRMAAXPgWTsfEFdspLg8NuNmDdHN_4NVZbUFNikbBJCzaXO8eMs1E_aBjDzU98GnMls3PLglh2WzrA4yak3sjClVqaIMrGxaBsr2WSRdjnO0LppJZvRFL5r4AFiRJsb6plKh698KPwJ4Xgyt343c6lZE8scM1kceGH87ZCtUCOHBkLiEYC-BrIm4zSCrOFhEsJXWJMMtgaLVYnSbt5CkgjOwGoU2IVDC9VSTv2VvzssabHpuVyOWMZnKkV14gYj9njwO3zTZvpZVWOX3xtXhJ5-33Rjlmc6H5CW6CcwZPFmHlZwcCXC3vxOt-HjbPubx3mfAIO3cBrF0HzqsTGLi-HZpS5SLgTQrIWrCRA3a0ftUv6fRpQ7kgJYByWt6lgfDWmx69SoC1PvSNjKC1djYEzbc8oT0iWual/9NCw5ewPZl75FQYc7W3roRQTQmqMPrz7/12)

clean_body: Women shouldn’t have to share locker rooms with men. | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this | | | ** Dear Defender of Truth, Women shouldn't have to share locker rooms with men. They shouldn't have to compete against male athletes. And they shouldn't be punished for speaking the truth. But across America, that's exactly what's happening. Men are being allowed to compete in women's sports--robbing girls of scholarships, medals, titles, and safety. Now, for the first time in history, the U.S. Supreme Court has agreed to hear two cases--State of West Virginia v. B.P.J. and Little v. Hecox --that could decide the future of women's sports nationwide. This could be a watershed moment in the fight to protect biological reality and fairness. If you believe women's sports should be reserved for women, we need your voice today. Your signature will say clearly: ✅ Men should not be allowed to compete on women's teams ✅ Women deserve privacy, fairness, and safety ✅ Radical gender ideology must not ignore biological truth After you sign, you'll be taken to the next critical step... Make a gift to support female athletes like Lainey Armistead, a courageous former college soccer player who is helping defend her state's Save Women's Sports Act in court. Your gift of any amount will help Alliance Defending Freedom stand strong at the Supreme Court--at no cost to our clients. 🚨 We've won 16 cases at the Supreme Court since 2011. With your help, and God's blessing, we can win again. Please add your name now--and then take the next bold step to protect women's sports with a generous gift. Together, we can ensure every girl has a fair opportunity to play--and win--on a level playing field. Thank you for standing for truth. With gratitude, Suzanne Beecher** Legal Counsel Alliance Defending Freedom 15100 N. 90th Street, Scottsdale, Arizona 85260

name: Erika Kirk
subject: My cries will echo around the world like a battle cry
body:
We will never surrender. Ever.

#
[https://secure.anedot.com/turning-point-usa/f8f86882-401a-4702-8c9b-df5abce81f1f?source_code=MAWCO69575&utm_medium=email&utm_source=hardask_acq&utm_campaign=ek&utm_term=MAWCO69575]

Friend,

I want to thank you for loving my husband.

For standing with us in the worst days of our lives. For praying. For
mourning. For remembering.

But now, _I need you to listen closely..._

BECAUSE THE EVILDOERS WHO TOOK CHARLIE’S LIFE? THEY HAVE NO IDEA
WHAT THEY HAVE DONE.

They didn’t just try to silence a man...

They tried to silence his message — a message of patriotism, of
faith, and of God’s merciful love.

Instead, they ignited something even greater.

IF THEY THOUGHT MY HUSBAND’S MISSION WAS POWERFUL BEFORE... _THEY
HAVE NO IDEA WHAT THEY HAVE JUST UNLEASHED._

NOT JUST ACROSS THIS COUNTRY... _BUT IN ME__._

They have no idea the fire they’ve lit inside this wife.

And to those listening across America... Let me be clear: The movement
my husband built will not die.

_I REFUSE TO LET THAT HAPPEN. _

And I know all of us will refuse to let that happen, too.

Charlie’s mission will grow: Stronger. Bolder. Louder. Greater than
ever.

I shared these words publicly, from my heart — just two days after I
lost the love of my life.

I WANT YOU TO WATCH. I WANT YOU TO HEAR WHAT CHARLIE MEANT TO ME. AND
I WANT YOU TO HEAR WHAT COMES NEXT:
[https://secure.anedot.com/turning-point-usa/f8f86882-401a-4702-8c9b-df5abce81f1f?source_code=MAWCO69575&utm_medium=email&utm_source=hardask_acq&utm_campaign=ek&utm_term=MAWCO69575]

#
[https://secure.anedot.com/turning-point-usa/f8f86882-401a-4702-8c9b-df5abce81f1f?source_code=MAWCO69575&utm_medium=email&utm_source=hardask_acq&utm_campaign=ek&utm_term=MAWCO69575]

Charlie gave everything for this country. And I will never stop
speaking his name.

We will not let the light he lit go out. Not now. Not ever.

For Charlie,

#
[https://secure.anedot.com/turning-point-usa/f8f86882-401a-4702-8c9b-df5abce81f1f?source_code=MAWCO69575&utm_medium=email&utm_source=hardask_acq&utm_campaign=ek&utm_term=MAWCO69575]

Erika Kirk

On behalf of the Turning Point USA Family

#
[https://secure.anedot.com/turning-point-usa/f8f86882-401a-4702-8c9b-df5abce81f1f?source_code=MAWCO69575&utm_medium=email&utm_source=hardask_acq&utm_campaign=ek&utm_term=MAWCO69575]

P.S. Charlie always said: _“Never surrender.”_

He built something bigger than himself — and now we must carry it
forward.

If you believe in what he stood for... if you refuse to let
Charlie’s vision die...

GIVE IN CHARLIE’S HONOR
SUPPORT TURNING POINT USA

4940 East Beverly Road
Phoenix, Arizona 85044

Unsubscribe: https://app.vbout.com/index.php?r=Email/OptoutConfirmationForm&messageid=319390&trkid=910033923&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com

215 N Payne Street, Alexandria, VA 22314

name: The Real Texas PAC
subject: 🚨BREAKING: Supreme Court Greenlights ICE Raids in Los Angeles
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Real Texas PAC, www
body:
🚨 BREAKING: The Supreme Court has just lifted restrictions that limited ICE activity in Los Angeles.
Those restrictions were originally put in place because federal agents were sweeping up U.S. citizens and targeting people based on race, language, job, or neighborhood.
Now, those protections are gone.
This means ICE has the green light to resume aggressive raids across Los Angeles — tearing families apart, intimidating immigrant communities, and sowing fear in everyday life. Civil rights groups are warning that this decision could spread nationwide if it goes unchallenged.
We need to know where you stand.
POLL: How concerned are you about the Supreme Court’s decision to expand ICE powers in Los Angeles?

name: Team DLCC
subject: 30+ wins, 3 flips, 5 majority-deciding elections
disclaimer_text: paid for by DLCC Victory Fund, 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005
body:
2025 has been a fantastic year for down-ballot Dems. With your help, we can keep that momentum going. | | |
---
| |
---|---
**State Democrats have won over _30 special elections_** **so far in 2025.** That’s over 30 new Democrats in state legislatures working to lower costs, expand health care access, and improve our education system. **We’ve flipped _3 seats_** **previously held by Republicans.** That’s three seats without MAGA members willing to sell their constituents out to an authoritarian regime. We even broke a GOP supermajority in Iowa, giving state Democrats the power to block MAGA appointees to government agencies. **_5 majority-deciding elections_****have gone our way.** By defending seats in the Virginia House and Senate, the Pennsylvania House, and the Minnesota House and Senate in special elections, Democrats have kept our majorities intact. [These elections were not won by chance. Can we count on you to chip in $7 to help our party build on these victories and help state Dems defeat extreme Republicans in the states? ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu05XtZnrBMT5R_mtAuAjxDFSMauhu6y669kwgxpsLsOxtfHm0UBaaTQBxY1RpnO-p-swejmhkSBfVVS2MGL6e37hCQp9h8UW5cnHn25YzSKB81Ph8YlmJfLgqxP6vON523BRgNb1GVXqXPLPwoyCtYNaQqOvFEpCWehuNC-vWNJ91R2AfkrMOOLnVSWXGLRyA7wIize8_vuq3LnYeO9nuZgFrjcGdDhZc6huaFhiVk2OwSFTH67vHYgbrMVkQL4ZpIJDjt2XuFkU8ntPfHmBoNh0h9eFP_weNsc-WNUO9Un-iCOjsCM113OGASQspIGQq65XJo4Z3Z8TWh1IiYWOXsY1WB_ZNY9SUtljRwVFfEWyDdCelYKd-tcvENVv3DrqXly1hypIDA8BjabVYpNfUaeCCo1qJwpxQZoonjNBtf2A/4jw/qjQUll5ySYCSuFLWL2-5Tg/h0/h001.aHebSciu7LgiZ0boZEUC6_n6yercr5AzHJ3BdIIz8a4) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately.

clean_body: 2025 has been a fantastic year for down-ballot Dems. With your help, we can keep that momentum going. | | | | | ---|--- State Democrats have won over 30 special elections so far in 2025. That’s over 30 new Democrats in state legislatures working to lower costs, expand health care access, and improve our education system. We’ve flipped 3 seats previously held by Republicans. That’s three seats without MAGA members willing to sell their constituents out to an authoritarian regime. We even broke a GOP supermajority in Iowa, giving state Democrats the power to block MAGA appointees to government agencies. 5 majority-deciding electionshave gone our way. By defending seats in the Virginia House and Senate, the Pennsylvania House, and the Minnesota House and Senate in special elections, Democrats have kept our majorities intact. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | | 2025 has been a fantastic year for down-ballot Dems. body:
They want us to give up. We’re not going anywhere.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/115212955/569559910/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-09-14_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI1MzUyMThkLTk4OTEtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=XUXuLsCSDj25p9aTh_FGr9B69BtY8kUemWAykTWMdSw=)

| Peter,

Republicans are banking on us being so overwhelmed by the nonstop chaos in the news that we stop fighting back. When they’re rigging maps and suppressing voters, they’re counting on us to give up.

But our September deadline is just a couple weeks away and right now, we’re still falling short of our goal. If we don’t close the gap, we’ll have fewer resources to train women, fewer programs to prepare them to run, and fewer leaders ready to take the fight directly to Republicans.

We cannot hand them that victory.

[_**Can you make your first contribution of $25 to help us train the women who will fight back?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/115212956/569559911/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-09-14_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI1MzUyMThkLTk4OTEtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=XUXuLsCSDj25p9aTh_FGr9B69BtY8kUemWAykTWMdSw=)

clean_body: They want us to give up. We’re not going anywhere. | = | Peter, Republicans are banking on us being so overwhelmed by the nonstop chaos in the news that we stop fighting back. When they’re rigging maps and suppressing voters, they’re counting on us to give up. But our September deadline is just a couple weeks away and right now, we’re still falling short of our goal. If we don’t close the gap, we’ll have fewer resources to train women, fewer programs to prepare them to run, and fewer leaders ready to take the fight directly to Republicans. We cannot hand them that victory. = | = Every dollar we raise in these next couple of weeks will go toward building the movement Republicans are desperate to stop. They want us to give up. We’re not going anywhere. Ashley Floyd Senior Development Director * | | = Paid for by Emerge America Emerge America 818 Connecticut Avenue NW, Suite 450 Washington, DC 20006 United States | = =

The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. **Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.**

body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1) This is **GETTING OUT OF HAND,** team →

In the name of “stopping crime”, Donald Trump ordered the military to take over D.C. – despite crime being at a **30-YEAR LOW!!!**

Donald Trump is wielding power like a DICTATOR, and this is just one more step toward full blown authoritarianism. [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=3&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1) [ **This hostile takeover could have been prevented if D.C. was a state...but if we’re going to convince Congress to act, we need to show them that Democrats across the country agree with us. ** ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=4&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1) [ **Team, if you believe that D.C. should become the 51st state, sign our massive petition now! →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=5&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1) | [SIGN THE PETITION >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=6&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1)
---
Here’s the honest truth about D.C., team:
1\. Over **700,000 Americans live in Washington, D.C.,** which is more than in Vermont or Wyoming. But they have ZERO representation in the Senate?!!

2\. A staggering **86% of D.C. residents** have already voted in favor of becoming the 51st state – a clear mandate for statehood and equal representation.

3\. If DC becomes a state, we’d gain two more Democratic Senators!! [ **That would give us a real shot at flipping the Senate and stopping Donald Trump’s MAGA agenda in its tracks.** ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=7&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1)
This is exactly why Donald Trump and the MAGA GOP are _fighting tooth and nail against D.C. becoming the 51st state!!_
[ **Supporters across the country are on board with making D.C. a state, but we need to know if local Democrats in your area agree... ** ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=8&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1)
[ **Team, if you support making D.C. the 51st state (and adding 2 Democratic Senators!!) add your name to our massive petition now! **](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=9&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1)
[SIGN THE PETITION >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/68770?t=10&akid=23398%2E4449941%2Ec9C6v1)
---
Counting on you,

Fight For Reform

name: Jordan Wood
subject: Insulted.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
I want to share something deeply personal with you.
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T86b3123c-6d66-4991-b566-2d77d2ed05b4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Peter, I want to share something deeply personal with you. Since I launched my Democratic campaign to replace GOP Senator Susan Collins, I’ve received such heartwarming support from so many of you. I am incredibly grateful. But to be honest, Peter, it hasn’t all been positive. Some are telling me I don’t deserve to run for office. Or serve in the Senate. Or even be a father – because I’m gay. [Please read this very closely, Peter: These hateful attacks never stopped me before, and you better believe they won't stop me from fighting for every type of family in the Senate now. Our latest poll shows me within ONE SINGLE point of flipping this critical Senate seat for Democrats – but a massive Super PAC just rushed Susan Collins an unprecedented $5.6 MILLION to take me down. I’m urgently asking 1,000 supporters to split a $3 donation between me and Blue Amp Action in the next 4 hours to help me rescue our shot to bring this win home and ensure every type of family has the representation they deserve in the Senate >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1761b193-d88b-4c30-9323-688c37f1b031/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T30b873bf-39e9-4163-9308-e075c280cef8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
_Jake, Jordan, and Ella_ I was in second grade when Susan Collins was first elected to the Senate. Now, 30 years later, my husband and I just became new dads to our baby girl, Ella – and Susan Collins is still in the Senate. I never planned to run for office myself — but after watching Collins make things *worse* for our families by voting for Trump’s dangerous cabinet, Supreme Court nominees, and even his ACQUITTAL — I looked at my daughter and made the pressing choice to fight for her future. Peter, no matter how many hateful attacks are thrown at me, I need you to know that nothing will deter me from fighting on your behalf in the Senate. [But the harsh reality is – unless I can compete with Susan Collins’ earth-shattering sum of cash, our chance to make history and gain a critical Senate seat for Democrats will be over. So I’m humbly asking 1,000 supporters to split a $3 donation between me and Blue Amp Action in the next 4 hours to close the fundraising gap and show the haters what we’re made of. Can I count on you to help me FLIP this Senate seat blue and hand Collins and her MAGA financiers the humiliating defeat they so desperately deserve? >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T52f81d96-6ff3-490f-bc13-9b1b09aa548f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [CONTRIBUTE $3](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tee7a6e53-6169-44c1-9b4e-9643455eebc0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [CONTRIBUTE $25](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc7c125f3-021d-4b2c-8515-ae39875885e3/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | [CONTRIBUTE MORE](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8c9c72ab-2a69-436e-88ad-3ee60d8cd3cf/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | | | Thank you, Jordan

PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208
---
| Having trouble viewing this email? [View it in your web browser](https://blueampstrategies.salsalabs.org/loc_em_fr_20250914_wood_b2_f1_s1_c1_warmup_rs-t0?wvpId=368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | |
---
| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te05f34c5-c635-488f-8201-5c13d832d935/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Team Sherrod
subject: this is what is at stake
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
| Sherrod never planned to run for office again. But when he saw what was happening in our country – politicians in Washington siding with billionaires and corporate interests, costs rising for working families, health care being ripped away – he knew he couldn’t stay on the sidelines. There’s simply too much at stake for Ohio families. Our battleground race is going to be one of the most competitive in the country, and to win, Sherrod needs supporters like you to step up from the very start. [**Will you split $10 between Sherrod and Seth Moulton now to help us win in Ohio?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115213213/569560833/473596456?refcode=250914_MFC_Sherrod_AtStake&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MjNkOTc1OS05YTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7XJmrXoB4ivPsr3J5ybX_2oi_zZFEUFTpJJ8QUg36Ls=) This race isn’t about left or right. It’s about whether Ohio workers have someone in the Senate fighting for them. Right now, no one is. Workers are more productive than ever, but wages are flat, costs are skyrocketing – and corporate special interests keep rigging the system. Sherrod couldn’t stand by and watch. Not while regular people were getting hurt. He’s getting back in the ring because he believes in the Dignity of Work – the idea that hard work should pay off for everyone, no matter who you are or what kind of work you do. [**But he can’t do it without you, friend: Will you chip in $10 split between Seth Moulton and Sherrod to win in Ohio and keep fighting for working families?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115213214/569560836/473596456?refcode=250914_MFC_Sherrod_AtStake&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MjNkOTc1OS05YTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7XJmrXoB4ivPsr3J5ybX_2oi_zZFEUFTpJJ8QUg36Ls=) Thank you. – Team Sherrod

| We can’t wait for permission.
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=4976%2E35263%2E0Kp_M1)
I’m used to being written off, Peter. When I first ran for Congress in 1997, nobody thought I would win. And when an extremely popular challenger jumped into the race, we faced an uphill battle. I remember huddling with one of my staffers at the time, and she said, _“Well Tammy, maybe you want to rethink what you’re doing because it’s going to be tough.”_ I looked her right in the eye and asked, “Do you think I should wait my turn?” The next thing I said was this: I believe that women have to run — especially when it’s hard — and not wait for permission. We can’t wait until we get a tap on our shoulder, or we’ll never get anywhere. I ended up winning that race and became the very first woman elected to Congress in Wisconsin. It wouldn’t be the last time this team would make history. **Because when I ran for U.S. Senate in 2012, folks like you fought alongside me — even as they wrote us off.** You helped me become the first woman elected to the chamber from our state and the first openly gay Senator in our nation’s history. In 2018 and 2024, despite being a top target for national Republicans both times, you stood with me again and we won re-election. Time and again, grassroots supporters like you have defied the odds. The fight to undo the damage that Donald Trump has caused and make real change in Washington will be no different. **[I’m reaching out to you today to ask that you help us meet our mid-September fundraising goal and help us build the movement that will continue to fight for what’s right. You can chip in right here:](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=stb-em-sep25.avzHzX&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250914_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=4976%2E35263%2E0Kp_M1)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $5 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/3663?c0=stb-em-sep25.i9k4bD&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250914_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=4976%2E35263%2E0Kp_M1)
---

Over the years, our team has weathered just about every storm — from outside groups spending millions against us to nasty right-wing attacks — only to get back up and keep fighting.
I’m so grateful to have you on this team.

name: SIGN NOW [VPP]
subject: “We’re going to fight fire with fire”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Gavin Newsom: “END TRUMP’S PRESIDENCY”
| **_LOOK! LOOK! LOOK!_
THIS IS HUGE!** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93908?t=6&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM) [**We’re calling on our 10,000 MOST LOYAL Democrats to sign our letter THANKING and standing with Gov. Newsom! Signature spots are running out, so sign NOW before Trump can silence him »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93908?t=7&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93908?t=8&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM)
---
TO: Governor Gavin Newsom
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank you for taking a stand. Dear Governor Newsom,

_I am THRILLED about your redistricting plan to CRUSH the MAGA House majority and end Trump’s presidency as we know it!_

I refuse to sit on the sidelines while Trump attempts to STEAL the 2026 midterms.

**Thank goodness we have you leading the charge to FIGHT BACK!**

I’m doing everything in my power to protect Democrats like you who are brave enough to face Trump head-on and risk MAGA revenge.

[**I know you’re facing relentless attacks from Trump and his MAGA minions, so I’m signing this thank you letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to help Democrats keep fighting for justice and WIN in 2026 »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93908?t=9&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93908?t=10&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM)
---
[](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93908?t=11&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM)
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21211%2E878179%2Esf46XM).**

name: Connor Miller
subject: Can you help us catch up?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
I’m the Finance Director for Sarah’s campaign.
#
Hi, it's Connor — I’m the Finance Director for Sarah’s campaign.
It’s my job to make sure we have the resources to compete next November and win back the House.
While the midterms might seem far away, our work has already started. And let me be honest, our fundraising is lagging where it should be right now. **On top of that, we have one of our _last_ FEC fundraising deadlines of the year coming up on September 30!**
To help us catch up, Sarah and I set a goal to raise $34,000 before midnight tomorrow, and right now we’re still $13,000 short.
**Supporters like you have always had Sarah’s back.** [**If you’re able, can you pitch in $5 or anything you can before tomorrow night’s mid-month deadline?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250914?refcode=em20250914-2334&amount=5&refcode2=2334_21716_Tucs5_&akid=2334%2E21716%2ETucs5_)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250914?amount=5&refcode=em20250914-2334&refcode2=2334_21716_Tucs5_&akid=2334%2E21716%2ETucs5_)**
Sarah counts on us to power this campaign so she can focus on advocating and legislating in Congress at a time when our freedoms and futures are on the line.
She doesn’t have billionaires on speed dial to fill the gap when our fundraising slows.
[**We rely on a grassroots team, and I know if everyone reading this email contributes just a little bit, we’ll get back on track! Can you chip in now?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250914?refcode=em20250914-2334&amount=5&refcode2=2334_21716_Tucs5_&akid=2334%2E21716%2ETucs5_)
Thanks. I’d love to tell Sarah that you, along with hundreds of other supporters, stepped up today!
—Connor
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20250914-1&t=1&refcode2=2334_21716_Tucs5_&akid=2334%2E21716%2ETucs5_)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=2&akid=2334%2E21716%2ETucs5_)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=3&akid=2334%2E21716%2ETucs5_)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2334%2E21716%2ETucs5_)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)
Paid for by McBride for Delaware

| | Peter, we can count on Austin Davis. He works tirelessly for the people of Pennsylvania, he's a fierce advocate for the Commonwealth, and he's proven himself as a leader we can rely on. We can count on Austin Davis to stand up to Trump. He and Gov. Josh Shapiro have taken him to court and won, securing funding for PA schools and fighting back against his overreach. We can count on him to stand firm in support of your rights and freedoms, whether in his record as a champion for pro-choice legislation or his steadfast commitment to our union brothers and sisters. We can count on him to combat economic inequality with targeted investments in our community, from public education to mass transit. He's made PA infrastructure a top priority and helped bring 11,000 new jobs to the Commonwealth. **We can count on Austin Davis, but can we count on _you_?** [**We're asking our most dedicated supporters to step up and chip in ahead of our final mid-month deadline of the quarter. We can count on him to stand firm in support of your rights and freedoms, whether in his record as a champion for pro-choice legislation or his steadfast commitment to our union brothers and sisters. body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115214605/569565643/1806897946?refcode=EM_F_250915EO_TSB_90DO_7_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1N2M0ODkzNi1hMTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lSxVXJvGvIz07D48Kg81o-m2nlGpr091RrqZvsP8UXo=) Peter,

**Donald Trump made opposing DEI initiatives one of the top priorities of his administration, and Shontel Brown has been leading the charge to fight back.**

[**Before we explain, will you help Shontel fight for a just and equitable nation by pitching in $5 today?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115214606/569565644/1806897946?refcode=EM_F_250915EO_TSB_90DO_7_NG_N_L1&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1N2M0ODkzNi1hMTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lSxVXJvGvIz07D48Kg81o-m2nlGpr091RrqZvsP8UXo=)

The Equal Employment Opportunity Executive Order 11246, signed by President Lyndon B. Johnson in 1965, prohibited employment discrimination by federal contractors. This was a fundamental civil rights measure that guaranteed equal opportunities for workers regardless of race, gender, color, or national origin.

This Executive Order has directly protected 10.3 million workers from discrimination through workplace investigations over the past decade and secured financial relief for more than 250,000 workers.

However, as one of his first actions in office, **Trump signed an Executive Order that ended anti-discrimination policies across the federal government.** By doing so, Trump has undermined the progress made in recent decades toward eliminating discrimination in federal contracting. As usual, this is just another attempt by the Trump administration to attack workers' rights and take the nation backward.

**We are fighting back:** Shontel Brown is taking charge to ensure we do not turn our backs on these communities – she is calling on the Department of Labor to reconsider the proposed rule and continue upholding the principles of this Executive Order.

[**Once again, Shontel is proving herself as a leader in the fight against the Trump regime. She isn't afraid to speak out and stand up for what’s right, including fair treatment for all employees. Right now, as I’m sending this email, my seat in Congress is at risk.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115214772/569566133/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTQ2ZGFhMS1hMTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t0R6VKx29xRhC8OjrRyRP_ZNBuMaMepr9eiUaxIY6JQ=) Right now, as I’m sending this email, my seat in Congress is at risk. Not because it’s an election year, but because extreme Republican politicians are taking direct marching orders from Washington. [_**I’m fighting an uphill battle against extreme politicians in the state legislature who want to gerrymander my seat and give Donald Trump total control over our state. Can you chip in to help me fight back?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115214773/569566134/-2097022923?refcode=email_091325_risk&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTQ2ZGFhMS1hMTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t0R6VKx29xRhC8OjrRyRP_ZNBuMaMepr9eiUaxIY6JQ=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115214774/569566135/-2097022923?refcode=email_091325_risk&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTQ2ZGFhMS1hMTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t0R6VKx29xRhC8OjrRyRP_ZNBuMaMepr9eiUaxIY6JQ=)

There’s a pretty obvious reason why National Republicans want me out of Congress: **I don’t fit their mold of what a “Kansas politician” should look like.**
And they’re right. I’m not a normal member of Congress. I wasn’t given a massive trust fund when I turned 18, and I wasn’t handed everything to me on a silver platter. I was raised by a single Army mom, paid my own way through college, and even had a career as an MMA fighter before I ran for elected office.
Maybe that background doesn’t make me a “typical” politician. **But I think that’s a good thing.** Because Congress doesn’t need any more billionaire apologists or career politicians. Washington needs people who know what it’s like to work hard for a living.
[_**Look, I don’t know how this gerrymandering battle will end. But I _do_ know I’m going to spend every waking second playing to win. If you’re with me, and you’re ready to keep this seat out of Donald Trump’s hands, will you chip in a donation to our campaign in the next 24 hours?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115214775/569566136/-2097022923?refcode=email_091325_risk&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTQ2ZGFhMS1hMTkxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t0R6VKx29xRhC8OjrRyRP_ZNBuMaMepr9eiUaxIY6JQ=)

clean_body: Right now, as I’m sending this email, my seat in Congress is at risk. = Right now, as I’m sending this email, my seat in Congress is at risk. Not because it’s an election year, but because extreme Republican politicians are taking direct marching orders from Washington. = | | = There’s a pretty obvious reason why National Republicans want me out of Congress: I don’t fit their mold of what a “Kansas politician” should look like. And they’re right. I’m not a normal member of Congress. I wasn’t given a massive trust fund when I turned 18, and I wasn’t handed everything to me on a silver platter. I was raised by a single Army mom, paid my own way through college, and even had a career as an MMA fighter before I ran for elected office. Maybe that background doesn’t make me a “typical” politician. But I think that’s a good thing. Because Congress doesn’t need any more billionaire apologists or career politicians. Washington needs people who know what it’s like to work hard for a living. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Together, we can win this fight for Kansas’ future. Thank you for having my back. Sharice | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Rebuilding the American Dream
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-header-2.png]

Friend,

My parents were working class Americans, sacrificing to get by like so many in Northwest Ohio. But they made a good life for us here, the same way so many Ohioans did: with hard work, perseverance, and the support of their community.

Thanks to the sacrifices they made and the values they passed down to me, I became the first member of our family to attend college. But today, that version of the American Dream is increasingly out of reach for too many people.

Friend, with the cost of higher education skyrocketing, a four-year degree cannot be the only path to prosperity in this country.

The people who make and fix the products that keep America running should be able to live with security and dignity.

That's why I'm fighting every day in Congress to invest in job training and apprenticeship programs, protect union jobs, and level the playing field for Ohio workers. Are you with me? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=250910_mk_skills_l1]

Support our work → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=250910_mk_skills_b1]With you,

— Marcy
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-headshot.jpg]

About Marcy Kaptur
Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. She’s worked with both parties to secure investments in our communities and has been a true friend of veterans, seniors, first responders and working people. She’s a voice for us all in the People’s House.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=emailfooter&refcode2=f3989854-a291-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413] Give Monthly [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?recurring=true&refcode=footermonthly&refcode2=f3989854-a291-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413]
Follow Marcy
https://twitter.com/Marcy_Kaptur [https://twitter.com/Marcy_Kaptur] https://www.facebook.com/marcykapturforcongress [https://www.facebook.com/marcykapturforcongress]
Checks may also be mailed to:
Kaptur for Congress - PO Box 899, Toledo, OH 43697 Paid for by Kaptur for Congress Email is a critical way to stay in touch with our supporters, but if you wish to unsubscribe, you may do so here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/tcAb-PaXtEayL-mWUU_QGw2?unsubscribedata=i58G%2FAnXi5YwZNQCw9bhpjWHV1MzN%2BjM8TAOasFM%2FyqDDPj4G2edT%2BGX7oY0ot5vWqQrsJrGiAqjT6FwWZfHTMdk9MKNUlY82wdt4QJJyEAd9EwJ1bgQ5Gwg0bWvVLIAwscI%2BKFhW%2Fu7YrpWWqEcwPJjYNn39erjGFrSN7SozlYVGQCM9n97Cn5Ck7u5TWusFDgq05FJ%2B7ec229Qji89mi5dhfCR9KiIutjs4p1fxntepRxAMqLCvzGV%2BOpEdsmyeDQKFnmAUI2hLgWbQCulnymA%2F2r7rZLGDH%2BviofS56zwNFACOWoK9hbAY%2B6KZhEDNefhaQF5av5xIhEMOyKW2xoO1lusfzf6pdF%2BDBqHwyIgIsxNXVw%2FWtt6FpOLKlPbpvmd6XcxpPaCqw24yJCsGmjOULfJEmxHgSZ2br2cpZQ%3D] . name: Gabby Giffords
subject: I hope you’ll take a minute to read this
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
I just wrote an op-ed for Time Magazine
#

Friends: after this week’s assassination of Charlie Kirk, I wrote an op-ed for TIME Magazine discussing the alarming rise of politically-motivated attacks and the need to keep our communities safe from gun violence.
We may never be able to eliminate all gun violence. But we must realize that all gun violence is preventable.
I’ve attached the op-ed below. Thank you for reading.
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250914-1-no&t=1&akid=10871%2E3935704%2EOPQ49m)**

* * *
[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250914-1-no&t=2&akid=10871%2E3935704%2EOPQ49m)
**Gabby Giffords:I Mourn for Charlie Kirk’s Family**
September 2025
[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250914-1-no&t=3&akid=10871%2E3935704%2EOPQ49m)
---
I mourn for Charlie Kirk’s family.
I didn’t agree with almost anything he said, but he had a right to speak. Just as he had a right to go on a work trip and return safely to his wife and two young children at home in the state we share, Arizona.
Just as Melissa Hortman, the Speaker of the Minnesota State Legislature, deserved to be safe at home with her husband and her dog. Instead they were all three shot dead together one night in June.
Just as President Donald Trump had the right to campaign without fear of being assassinated, as two different people tried to do last summer.
Just as I had the right to meet with my constituents safely on January 8, 2011 — the day when instead I, a young congresswoman in a purple district, was nearly assassinated. Eighteen other people were shot and six were killed.
Our stories are unique, but what Charlie Kirk, President Trump, Melissa Hortman and I all have in common is that someone who wanted to kill us had a gun.
We can and should talk about political violence, and its toxic relationship to political rhetoric. We can and must talk about social media’s role in these moments. We all, as individual Americans, need to do a better job considering our words and their impact. But anyone who responds to preventable tragedies like this—tragedies that over time begin to erode the very fabric of our country—by refusing to face the problem of gun violence and crime head-on is missing the point.
What we share, and what puts all of us in danger—from elected leaders to little children, like those shot while praying in church in Minnesota a few weeks ago—is the overwhelming prevalence of guns in this country and the loopholes that make it appallingly easy for dangerous people to access them.
In America, we now have more guns than people. Many states take sensible steps—background checks, extreme risk protection orders, and safe storage laws—all policies that help reduce gun crime and gun deaths. Other states—like Utah, which year after year receives a F grade in the annual Gun Law Scorecard released by my organization, GIFFORDS—do far too little to save lives. Utah has expanded gun access in recent years, and gun deaths have increased along with that, soaring by 45% from 2014 to 2023, according to the CDC.
I was gutted to learn that Charlie Kirk died yesterday in Utah—and it’s also true that in Utah, someone dies from gun violence every 20 hours. Too many families know the pain of having a loved one killed by guns, in that state and across the country.
In fact, mere hours after Kirk’s shooting, at least two students were shot at a high school in Evergreen, Colorado.
Americans of both political parties desperately want this violence to end. Our research suggests that the vast majority of Republican voters want to close loopholes that allow criminals and dangerous people to get their hands on guns. When you ask parents what they want most for their children’s education, they tell us again and again that keeping their kids safe from gun violence is their top priority. The American people are far more united than their representatives in Washington. Our polling shows 88% of Republicans support background checks on all gun sales, 75% support laws to get untraceable ghost guns off the streets, and a surprising 81% support requiring a license to own a gun (just like Americans need licenses to drive cars). Yet somehow Congress refuses to act.
It is physically very hard for me to speak due to my injuries from the bullets that hit me that day in January, and after watching yet another political shooting violently steal someone’s life before our eyes, it’s even harder to find words. So I want to speak plainly to my former colleagues in Congress, both the House and the Senate: Do something. Take action. Pray also if you want, but understand: your job is action. Nobody goes to the polls to elect someone to pray for them. We vote because we trust the people we elect will pass sensible, moderate policies that make our country a better, safer place to live and raise a family.
We may never be able to eliminate all gun violence. But we must realize that all gun violence is preventable. We know what to do to make our country safer for all of us, and our elected representatives can do it immediately. Come to the table, as Republicans and Democrats, to pass solutions to the epidemic of gun violence that’s killing both of us. There’s no shortage of policies with a history of bipartisan support: closing background check loopholes, supporting violence intervention programs, funding law enforcement—including the brave agents at the ATF who catch violent criminals and crack down on the small percentage of gun dealers who break the law—among many others. Above all: prioritize American lives over the donations and profits of gun industry CEOs.
When I was shot, my friend Judge John Roll was killed. He was a Republican. We didn’t always agree, but we still worked together for the good of our state. Also killed was Christina-Taylor Green. She was only nine years old and had just been elected Class President at her elementary school. Their families will never be whole again, just as the Kirk family will never be whole again, and I hold them all in my heart.
I call on my colleagues in Washington to show courage, not cowardice. I call on them to show loyalty to our country and our common values, and to do the single most important thing they can for their constituents: keep them safe.
* * *

Please, take a second to read this...
As a victim of political violence, Gabby Giffords has led the fight to save more lives from gun violence for more than a decade, but she can’t do it alone.
If everyone reading this donated just $17, we'd have the resources to pass commonsense gun safety legislation across the country. But not everyone gives — making it even more important for people who care and understand the stakes of our struggle to pitch in what they can.
**If $17 seems worth it to you to change our gun laws in order to make our communities safer from gun violence, we're asking you to contribute to GIFFORDS PAC today.**
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/61660?c0=giffords-em-sep25.Kx2kIW&refcode=em250914-1-no&t=4&akid=10871%2E3935704%2EOPQ49m)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=10871%2E3935704%2EOPQ49m) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

Many states take sensible steps—background checks, extreme risk protection orders, and safe storage laws—all policies that help reduce gun crime and gun deaths. Other states—like Utah, which year after year receives a F grade in the annual Gun Law Scorecard released by my organization, GIFFORDS—do far too little to save lives. Utah has expanded gun access in recent years, and gun deaths have increased along with that, soaring by 45% from 2014 to 2023, according to the CDC. I was gutted to learn that Charlie Kirk died yesterday in Utah—and it’s also true that in Utah, someone dies from gun violence every 20 hours. Too many families know the pain of having a loved one killed by guns, in that state and across the country. In fact, mere hours after Kirk’s shooting, at least two students were shot at a high school in Evergreen, Colorado. Americans of both political parties desperately want this violence to end. Our research suggests that the vast majority of Republican voters want to close loopholes that allow criminals and dangerous people to get their hands on guns. When you ask parents what they want most for their children’s education, they tell us again and again that keeping their kids safe from gun violence is their top priority. The American people are far more united than their representatives in Washington. Our polling shows 88% of Republicans support background checks on all gun sales, 75% support laws to get untraceable ghost guns off the streets, and a surprising 81% support requiring a license to own a gun (just like Americans need licenses to drive cars). Yet somehow Congress refuses to act. It is physically very hard for me to speak due to my injuries from the bullets that hit me that day in January, and after watching yet another political shooting violently steal someone’s life before our eyes, it’s even harder to find words. So I want to speak plainly to my former colleagues in Congress, both the House and the Senate: Do something. Take action. Pray also if you want, but understand: your job is action. Nobody goes to the polls to elect someone to pray for them. We vote because we trust the people we elect will pass sensible, moderate policies that make our country a better, safer place to live and raise a family. We may never be able to eliminate all gun violence. But we must realize that all gun violence is preventable. We know what to do to make our country safer for all of us, and our elected representatives can do it immediately. Come to the table, as Republicans and Democrats, to pass solutions to the epidemic of gun violence that’s killing both of us. There’s no shortage of policies with a history of bipartisan support: closing background check loopholes, supporting violence intervention programs, funding law enforcement—including the brave agents at the ATF who catch violent criminals and crack down on the small percentage of gun dealers who break the law—among many others. Above all: prioritize American lives over the donations and profits of gun industry CEOs. When I was shot, my friend Judge John Roll was killed. He was a Republican. We didn’t always agree, but we still worked together for the good of our state. Also killed was Christina-Taylor Green. She was only nine years old and had just been elected Class President at her elementary school. Their families will never be whole again, just as the Kirk family will never be whole again, and I hold them all in my heart. I call on my colleagues in Washington to show courage, not cowardice. I call on them to show loyalty to our country and our common values, and to do the single most important thing they can for their constituents: keep them safe. Please, take a second to read this... As a victim of political violence, Gabby Giffords has led the fight to save more lives from gun violence for more than a decade, but she can’t do it alone. If everyone reading this donated just $17, we'd have the resources to pass commonsense gun safety legislation across the country. But not everyone gives — making it even more important for people who care and understand the stakes of our struggle to pitch in what they can. If $17 seems worth it to you to change our gun laws in order to make our communities safer from gun violence, we're asking you to contribute to GIFFORDS PAC today. Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
